lines.x: No, Madame Oni! I can't let you go any further!
I have to kill that man.
The oyabun told me himself, I can't let you get any closer!
There's no time... Out of the way, Jirou. Last warning.
Madame Oni!!!
Even you're getting in my way now, Jirou?
I know I can't. I just want one last drink with you, okay?
I'm not trying to buy time, just... just one drink. When we're done, we can get back to killing each other.
The yakuza pulls out a bottle of sake and pours two cups, while the woman known as 'Madame Oni' silently watches on.
Drink already.
Madame Oni lifts her head, the sake falling straight into her belly, but rather than a gentle warmth, a raging fire burns in her like a twisted knife.
Jirou! You—!
I've got to protect him.
He might be your enemy, but he's always been good to me.
If he dies here, he's only going to be my benefactor. If he lives, he'll become the benefactor for all of Mitsukue. Sorry, Madame Oni.
GRAAAAAAAAAAAH!!!
The pain in her belly becomes a raging inferno that churns together with the fury in her mind, bursting its way out of her chest.

Unlike the chivalrous yakuza who walked against the flow, who knew right from wrong and believed in the justice she had always upheld, now, she has devolved into a vicious Oni, one who knows nothing but slaughter.

The Hannya in her hands can no longer be controlled by the great wisdom in scripture it is named for. All that is left is a frenzied fury that burns hotter than the face of Hannya, uncaring of former friendships.
Hyaaah!
Madame Oni?!
Gugh!
Stop her—
Whoa!
S-She's gone mad—
All who get in my way... must die.
Time, transformation, the general welfare...
These words must ring hollow to you. Belaboring the subject would not bring you redemption...
Vicious Oni.
Shut up...
SHUT UPPPPPPP!!!

lines.st: Like A Yakuza: Birth of the Vicious Oni
Starring
'Oni'—Momoka Hanyuu

lines.x: Excuse me.
No pushing! Are you trying to flatten everyone or something?
That giant uchiwa you've got, lift it up high!
Uchiwa?
Y'know, a fan you wave to show how big a fan you are. Isn't that board you got there a real big one? Making it a triangle's pretty creative. How long has Momo-cchi been your oshi?
Momo-cchi?
...Sorry, just trying to get through. I'm in a hurry.
Momoka! Momoka! Momoka!
Thank you everyone! Thank you so much!
I am so honored that my first role on the big screen got to be the leading lady in Birth of the Vicious Oni—the Nameless Oni!
I've always been fascinated by the Nameless Oni, as she's the subject of a kaidan tale that's been going around Mitsukue for ages.
An Oni with a single horn who rebels in the name of vengeance, wiping out an entire yakuza family in a berserk rage, before finally killing the candidate for the Diet she swore a blood feud against.
(Blood feud?)
But after realizing just how bloodstained her hands had become, she couldn't bear the guilt, ultimately committing suicide.
(Suicide?)
Her endless resentment meant she couldn't be reborn, so she became a specter roaming the streets. Vowing to bury the truth in the dark, she suffers no witness to live...
(Has the story really grown that much...?)
It's a terrifying and tragic story, but it's also a touching one. And now, it's finally out on the silver screen for all to see!
This movie means a lot to me. The people of Kajimachi were so kind to me when we were filming there, so I'd like to thank them while I have the chance.
I'd also like to take this opportunity to talk about Shin Onibugyou, coming out this winter!
I'll be playing the lead Onihappou-no-Take, giving everyone a whole new take on the Onibugyou story.
It'll have a few Birth of the Vicious Oni-related easter eggs and references too. I really can't tell you too much right now... but I hope you all go and watch it too!
Next, we have our very last song for this show, the ED for Birth of the Vicious Oni...
Kishoumon Nocturne, hope you all like it!
It is a slow song. The moment the music starts, the crowd quiets down.

The singer has already belted out an entire concert's worth of songs, the beads of sweat running down her neck just barely perceptible, but she strives to keep her breathing steady, singing to the gentle violin.
...Plot of the film aside, the song's pretty good. This 'Momo-cchi' has a great singing voice too.
Shame I don't have time for a break right now.
Hoshiguma turns her head towards the perimeter of the crowd.
Peering over the heads of hundreds, no, thousands of fans, she is looking to spy a target before the target spies her—
Unfortunately, at that very moment, they lock eyes.
There she is!
Gah!
'Scuse me, I need to switch places.
Eh? Wha—?
If you're not going to move, I'll push my way through.
I don't understand—
Sorry, pal.
The Oni woman lifts her 'uchiwa' up high. It is only at this point that the young fan notices the cold glint of steel shining off it.
Before he even has time to react, he is pushed aside by some monstrous force. The tall Oni has barged past him, and now she is only a few steps away from the stage.
Alone in this land, we all float on by♪
Our fleeting tender moments, cause such deep sighs♪
Let me forever gaze into your eyes♪
Looking at life's dawn and dusk, so filled with dreams♪
Thank you, everyone! Thank you all for listening!
That was the last song I have for you today—
Encore! Encore! Encore!
Momoka! Momoka! Here! Over here!
Amidst the surging crowd of fans, one is particularly excited. She lifts something up high, having done all she can to squeeze through the crowd to reach the stage.
Momoka! I-I live in Kajimachi, I was even an extra in Birth of the Vicious Oni.
This is a picture you took with us extras when we were wrapping up filming. I always wanted to give it to you, but never had the chance... Please, take it!
Kajimachi? You're...
Even Kajimachi has star-chasers like her these days? I thought only old geezers lived there—
As she muses, Hoshiguma inadvertently spots out the corner of her eye several men, very much not dressed like fans, heading towards the Kajimachi girl.
They don't look like plainclothes security either. She might even say they look more like... yakuza?
Just as they are about to grab hold of her, Momoka suddenly pulls her up onto the stage.
WHOOOOOOOO! MOMOKA!
The fans, lost to hype, rush the stage, making the already-cramped venue even more impenetrable.
Up on stage, the sparkling idol cups her hands around her fan's.
I-I-I... Y-Y-You...
Could you tell me your name?
R-Right! Kichisei! I'm Kichisei!
I'm sure I've seen you before...
You remember me? Tha—, well, wh-what did I even do that was so memorable, Momoka-san?
Hmm... This might be embarrassing, but you're the first person I've met who actually likes film set bentos...
That's because the rice is so good—Wait, wait... You saw that? No, nonono...
I put my own on the table next to you, so I got to watch you happily eat your sixth box.
(Deep bow) I'm so sorry—!
No, please, there's no need, I just meant—
As the girl leans forward, she loses her balance, stumbling into Momoka's embrace. Seeing her give one of her fans a big hug, the crowd goes wild.
Hey now, watch your step.
So, how is Kajimachi right now? Is everyone doing okay?
Kajimachi... Right now, it... It...
What?
The pachinko parlor's not seeing as much business as it used to. Most people who want to stay out of trouble have moved away, and they're shooting Shin Onibugyou in a big fancy sound stage studio...
No, wait, what I'm trying to say is that I'm here on Kajimachi's behalf to invite you back! Everyone would love to see you again!
Eh? Really? I'll gladly go, and I'll gladly see what I can do! I'll do anything I can for Kajimachi's sake!
(Whisper) Kichisei-san, security's almost here. You can leave from the side.
(Whisper) Th-Thank you, Momoka-san!
THANKS FOR THE PHOTO!
Momoka turns to look at her backup band. The lead keyboardist gives her a nod.
Then... Thank you everyone for your passion and energy! It really means a lot to me!
So I'd like to ask everyone to wait a little bit longer, and let the band have a moment to catch their breath.
I'll be back on stage in two minutes to give you all the perfect curtain call!
The venue lights slowly fade out.
But the darkness does not stop the crowd's fervent chatter. Hoshiguma takes the opportunity to haul herself up on stage, trying to leave the venue as fast as possible.
Who just stepped on me?!
Ugh.
Who're you?
(Isn't this one of the guys chasing Kichisei? Guess they really were plainclothes security.)
Just looking to head out, nothing else. It's too crowded down there.
Don't you fans pay a lot of money to be in a crowd like that?
Wait, I bet you're the type of messed-up fan who goes onstage to bother the talent!
I really am just trying to head out—
You really didn't check who's in charge of Momoka-san's agency?! Stay right there!
Hey, what the—?! The hell is this thing... A metal shield?!
Report, we've got trouble! There's someone on the stage—
Going to have to ask you to shut it.
I need to get out of here quick. If I end up chased by both security and the police—
I'm coming back on stage now, thank you everyone for waiting—
The lights turn on.
The two people up on stage look at each other with surprise, an unconscious man slumped on the floor between them.
An Oni with... a single horn?!
From the legend? The 'Nameless Oni' kaidan legend?!
Eek! G-Get back! Don't haunt me!
Stay away from meeeeeee!!!
As the crowd looks on, the one-horned Oni turns towards backstage, while Momoka huddles against the floor, shielding her head with her hands.
The lighting director cuts the lights, but it's no use. It only takes five seconds of silence before the venue devolves into complete bedlam.
*phew*... Finally got out of there.
Even if you account for the chaos, the encore should be over by now. The police will be on my trail soon enough.
Should I go meet them?
No, I should at least head back to Kajimachi first to see the old man. Who knows how much the place has changed in twenty years.
Ding, dong, ding, Lingones Department Store♪
Everything you want, you'll find it here, Lingones Department Store♪
(...Lingones Department Store? Wasn't it called Yamazakiya?)
(Did they extend the store so much it blocked the road?)
(Looks like I need to go inside to pass through.)
Why don't you come in for a drink? You look like you can handle your liquor, and we just got some high-quality whiskey—
Or if you would prefer a more old-school choice, we also have a fine collection of imo shochu...
No thank you.
(Behind the store, there should be—)
(Blocked up too?)
Take this, ojou-san.
No need.
No, you definitely need it. You need to be guided to the most precious thing on Terra, the stepping stone to 'happiness'.
A flier for a host club? I definitely don't need this.
Ah, whatever, it just seemed like you were looking for something. Anything I can help with?
What did you happen to lose here in this bustling street of entertainment?
The way home.
I never expected valiant ladies like you to have days when they were lost. But no matter—
Were all the alleys from Nita Street to Kajimachi blocked off? Can you only go using the main road now?
No need to be so hasty in going back—
Looks like there wasn't any point in asking.
They're still scanning the crowd... They haven't seen me yet.
Hoshiguma finds a perfectly unremarkable wall to sit down against. True enough, two police officers keep their eyes on the crowd, walking right past her without a second glance.

Most of the credit goes to the people she is sharing the wall with, a majority of them drunken office workers. Some of them lean against the wall to vomit, while others simply hunch over to puke onto the street.

As for those who want to entertain themselves in the entertainment district through means outside of alcohol, they appear as if on an infinite conveyor belt, a near-infinite supply of them on an endless loop.
She is pretty sure she saw this group of people walk past her not five minutes ago.
Just about time to go.
Taishou, that one-horned Oni... Are you sure she isn't the kaidan from the legend here to haunt me?
The kaidan from the tales aren't like the ghosts you're thinking of, relax.
B-But what else could that have been? A ghost is the only explanation...
If she was a ghost, why'd she run off without you doing anything?
Hmm...
If she didn't follow you, then the only explanation is pure coincidence. She's got nothing to do with you.
You're better off worrying about other things. From what you said, Mio-san would have to take responsibility for the big ruckus today.
Mio-san? No, her job is to keep me safe, and I'm fine, at least.
Anyway, just in case the ghost is real... It's not like anyone upstairs can blame her for failing to stop a ghost, right?
*sigh*
Here. Your chikuwa, daikon, and burdenbeast tendon.
The radiant Momoka on stage is now just a young woman venting her frustrations, sniffling back tears before using one hand to lift her mask...
And her other to bring a bamboo skewer to her mouth, biting off a chunk of chikuwa to chew on.
Phew.
I'm still scared, Taishou—
(I'll wait here at this yatai for a bit.)
(...Smells good.)
Taishou, I'll have a boiled fowlbeast egg, a piece of atsu-age, and a kombu knot.
Coming right up.
I just need to head that way to get to Kajimachi, right?
That's right. Got work there, okyaku-sama?
Just dropping by for a visit—
YOU! YOU'RE THAT—
Taishou! Look! Sh-She really did follow me!
Well—
I'm begging you, please don't haunt me, ghost-san! When I was playing you, I really really REALLY did my best! If you're not happy with my performance, I can ask for a reshoot—
Wait, no! The film's already out, it's too late for that!
I'm actually really scared of kaidan, ghost-san! I didn't mean to offend you!
Who's shouting? Is something going on over there?
You really led us on a wild fowlbeast chase, Hoshiguma-san. Still going to resist arrest?
Hardly. I wasn't planning on resisting in the first place.
Hands where I can see them.
Huh?
What just happened?
What do you mean, 'what just happened'?
I must be dreaming...
Why did the police just... arrest the ghost?
Here's your fried meatchop and rice, detective.
Good job. Go get some rest.
Thank you!
It's past midnight already. You've been hiding from us ever since you got to Mitsukue this afternoon. You must be hungry, you haven't eaten at all.
(Nods)
Alright then. Here, a fried meatchop with rice, Higashinese specialty.
The detective puts extra emphasis on the word Higashinese and pushes the tray toward Hoshiguma.
With nary a word, Hoshiguma picks up the bowl and chopsticks and begins shoveling rice down her gullet.
Well? How is it? Do they have food like this in Lungmen?
(Continues shoveling rice)
Would you look at that. Tell me, just how long has it been since you've enjoyed a proper Higashinese meal?
(Shoves chop into mouth)
Whatever... eat up.
(Noisily chews and crunches cabbage)
(Gulps water)
(Tilts head back and dumps remaining contents of bowl into mouth)
(Puts down empty bowl)
Phew.
Tastes like home, doesn't it?
It's not a home or nostalgia thing, Detective-san. I'm just hungry.
Why you——

Actually, I had a fried meatchop with rice right before I left Lungmen. Next time, could you get me something else? Like some oden from the place you dragged me out of? It smelled amazing—I didn't even get a sip of the broth.
And for the record, when I was footing the bill to feed suspects back in Lungmen, I always ordered cheongfun.
I suggest you start taking this a bit more seriously, Hoshiguma. You're the suspect this time.
Me? I haven't even been in Mitsukue for twenty-four hours. What could you possibly suspect me of?
A suspect in a case from twenty years ago isn't a suspect?
Detective-san...
Detective Koretou.
Detective Koretou.
Yes?
After twenty years, we're well past the statute of limitations.
Besides, you should be about done authenticating my Lungmen inspector's badge, no?
We haven't gotten the results back yet.
Let's assume you're telling me the truth. What would motivate a Lungmen cop like you to come all the way back here?
Did you lose your job? You have some enemies you need to deal with?
Did someone in Lungmen send you here?
Or are you perhaps connected to those rumors about the spy of the Northern Court?
Stop, stop, stop. If you keep throwing out guesses like that, this whole conversation is gonna spiral...
Take a look at this letter, Detective.
We just searched you—where the hell were you hiding that?
It takes a detective to outsmart a detective.
Phew.
Let's run another lap. How about it, partner? Think you can keep up with me?
Looks like you're good. Let's go.
(Is that the lady's car?)
The single-horned oni lays across the vehicle, sizes up the end of the straightaway, then charges dead ahead.
The howling winds make it hard for her to tell just how far her pursuers are.
What brings you here, ma'am?
I'm just stopping by. I don't plan on staying long.
Yenwu told me that before Swire set out for New Siesta, she tidied up all internal affairs, to lighten the load for those of you left behind.
Yeah. If Missy hadn't wrapped things up before leaving, how could I have found the time to come here?
That's true. Then what is it that's troubling you, Inspector Hoshiguma?
You can see it in my face, huh?
I wouldn't say so.
However, if repeatedly pushing the pedal to the metal still leaves you wrestling with anxiety, you shouldn't bottle all that emotion up on your own.
You and I are both Higashinese. When Little Ch'en and Swire aren't here, I have to act in their stead.
We are... aren't we?
Indeed we are. Higashinese.
I can't hide anything from you, ma'am. I recently received a letter...

lines.st: 'Dear Madam,

Summer's end is nigh, yet the stubborn heat still lingers—please mind your good health. Twenty years have passed with nary a word between us. Much has transpired—things best left unwritten. Yet I feel compelled to write to you, though I regret any trouble this may cause.
This letter is written for no other reason than the decade or so that we spent together in our hometown of Kajimachi, which is soon to vanish from the map.
Though the land is unaware, its people still have feelings; therefore, I have humbly taken it upon myself to organize the funeral rites for Kajimachi, which will be held soon.
In my waning years, to see old friends once more would be a grand blessing.
Sincerely,
Tessai'

lines.x: What a solemn, I mean, traditional letter.
This is the man who arranged for your refuge in Lungmen—the so-called 'father' of Hannya?
That's right. I called him Uncle Tessai. It's been so many years, he must be in his seventies or eighties by now.
I lived in Kajimachi before I left Mitsukue.
And so, is it not only proper to return to Kajimachi one last time, before it is no more?

To be honest, when I first came to Lungmen, I considered settling here for good. I thought about moving elsewhere if things didn't work out, even leaving Yan altogether, the same way I left Higashi—but the one thing I never considered was going back home.

It's been twenty years. I don't even know what Mitsukue is like these days. Instead of going back home, I might as well come here for a ride and to grab a plate of street food—stir-fried noodles—to put my mind at ease.
Don't you have a good reason to go home? The funeral, no?
You've volunteered to serve as a mighty guardian of Lungmen, throwing yourself into the fight without hesitation. Yenwu, myself, Lin, and all your friends—we've seen it with our own eyes.
But you shouldn't limit yourself. You're more than that.
Little Ch'en is gone, and Yühsia has more or less taken over for her father. One L.G.D. Commissioner alone won't keep this operation afloat.
I have neglected my responsibilities.
Nonsense.
Before Little Ch'en left Lungmen, you had words with her. They eventually reached my ears as well, whether I wanted to hear them or not.
Even if the problems you've bottled up inside can never be resolved, simply going back to have a chat with him while he still walks among the living is worth your while, yes?
You're right.
By the way, don't forget to bring back a few packs of proper Higashinese senbei for me?
Senbei?
A 'jianbing' shop opened up near the L.G.D. I thought it was Higashinese senbei, with the name translated, so I went and placed an order.

It wasn't until the piping-hot pancake came out—stuffed with fowlbeast egg, crispy meat, and scallions—that I realized it wasn't a senbei at all, but a Jiangqi jianbing. These are completely different dishes.
I think we've resolved any and all misunderstandings between us, right Detective Koretou?

The oyabun of the Kinsekikai yakuza clan sent you a letter, inviting you to the funeral in Kajimachi, his turf. And after some persuading from a particular individual of similar origin, you've decided to go back there—am I right?
That's right.
This particular individual...
Her identity is not important.
Is it not? If it was not for her, would you dare walk the streets of Mitsukue?
Of course I would!
Alright. If it really isn't important, then you can stay here a good twenty-four hours.
The station's quiet these days anyway. Hardly any of the calls we get are real emergencies.
Then what are they about?
They're reporting signs of a kaidan they want me to investigate.
A kaidan...? Like an urban legend? Ghost story?
Ha. You really haven't been back to Mitsukue for a while, huh?
This place doesn't even feel like a real police station any more—the townies have been treating us like cops from the Spiritual Affairs Station for a while now...
Who is it?
There's someone outside for you... it's... Mifune-san.
The weary-looking detective glances at Hoshiguma, then out the door; hatred is seared across his face.
If you're in the middle of something here, do you want me to stall him for a bit?
You? Stall him? You won't even get three words in before he calls someone else—and then that call will be routed straight to us.
Send him in——
Before the detective can finish speaking, a tall Phidia strides into the room. Ignoring the officers around him, he makes a beeline for Hoshiguma.
Kinbee?!
Never fear, Hoshiguma. As long as I'm here, the police can't touch you.
(Laughs dryly) Ha, ha. Kouhei Mifune-san—been a while since someone called you by your old name, from before you made it big, huh?
Well, why don't you try it then?
Good. Knowing when to yield is a virtue.
Mifune——
Hoshiguma, you will always address me as Kinbee.
Come. Let's go home.
Detective Koretou still hasn't——
Still hasn't what? Hasn't explained the basis for this arbitrary detention? Don't tell me this is about that case from twenty years ago?
What a pity—the statute of limitations already expired, many years ago actually. The Northern Court may have revised its penal code, but that doesn't give you southerners the right to bend the law.
Spare me the mudslinging. Even if the statute of limitations is up——

Yes, the statute of limitations is up, so on what grounds are the police still detaining people? I want a justification—one that can withstand a Diet inquiry, a televised hearing, and the court of public opinion.
This is intimidation!
This is me exercising my rights under the law!
Detective Koretou!
...Let them go.
Kinbee, Uncle Tessai sent you to get me?
The entire Kinsekikai is awaiting your return. Let's go. Stop arguing with the cops.
They exit the police station, turn right, and cross the street.
Nita Street unfolds before Hoshiguma, in broad daylight.
The harsh white sun shines down on the street, draining the buildings of their neon shimmer and baring the raw concrete beneath.

The morning commute is in full swing. Expressionless office workers hurry past shops that are either still closed or trying half-heartedly to drum up business—none of them look nearly as glamorous as they did under the moonlight.
Who was it that threw up on the street last night? Which man was persuaded to empty his wallet at the club? Under the light of day, they all look the same.
How many years has it been?! How're things in Lungmen? Still good? We hadn't heard a peep from you all this time. I was afraid something had happened to you. I was worried sick!
I figured, after all this time, nobody thought of me as one of the Kinsekikai anymore.
How? No matter how fate plays its tricks or how long you've been incommunicado—as long as you come back, then you're still family.
Over the past two decades, has the Kinsekikai ever issued you an 'expulsion' or 'no-contact' notice? Never.
Say what you will, but even now, at every official meeting, the seat to the Old Man's left is still left empty. You've always been the Kinsekikai's wakagashira—the heir apparent.
And you...?
Why, I'm just the wakagashira-hosa, the wakagashira's second.
Don't for a minute think the Old Man's gone senile. Everyone agrees with his decision.
If you hadn't offed that Diet candidate twenty years ago, before I could get to him too, the Kinsekikai would've been driven out of Kajimachi—crushed under the weight of his public pressure campaign.
Then, you made sure the rest of us were in the clear, taking all the heat for the killing. You fled to Lungmen alone, which allowed the Kinsekikai to survive.
You've sacrificed so much for us. If we just turned our backs on you, what kind of family would that make us?
Don't say such things.
It's all facts, clear as day, so why can't I state the facts?
Kinbee, we can talk ancient history later.
Uncle Tessai mentioned in his letter that he was holding a funeral for Kajimachi. Do you know about this?
A what?!
A funeral...? No... What are we talking about with a funeral?
Madame Oni, we really don't have time to wander the streets right now. We need to get back to Kajimachi and find him as quick as we can to straighten all this out.
The tall Phidia hastens his steps and hurries off towards Kajimachi.
He walks so fast, and with such long strides, that Hoshiguma has to break into a jog just to keep up.
(Yawns)
Ooo! New senbei flavors! 'Premium muskmelon with Siracusan red sauce' and 'condensed cystymilk with honjouzou soy sauce'...
They look delicious.
No. I'm counting my calories! I have to watch what I eat!
Huh? Mio-san?
I'll be at the agency in just a few minutes. Did you need something?
What?
Almost there. I'm running over now!
'Treating fans like fools, nose in the air'...
'I'm no fan of hers anymore, that's for sure. She ruined the concert!'...
'Trying so hard to go viral, pretending to be scared—aren't you embarrassed?'...
What... what is all this?
A media smear campaign. It started early yesterday morning—most likely the work of a rival.
Producer.
Producer! I didn't know you were coming!
What's the situation?
It all happened so suddenly. PR is still investigating.
This isn't just PR.
What do you mean?
That 'one-horned Oni' wasn't our doing, right?
Mio, you are responsible for Momoka's safety. Please get to the bottom of all this.
Of course.
Momoka, your gaffe on stage yesterday was one thing, but upstairs is more upset about something else.
What's that...?
You've been acting less and less like a pop idol lately. Then this happens, and you vanish without a trace?
The fans didn't seem to react too negatively. I even gave them an encore. They were into it.
I was tired and hungry when I hopped offstage, not to mention scared. I just went to my favorite oden yatai, is all.
Well, how do you explain your inappropriate monologue up there?
'Inappropriate monologue'?! What, what did I say wrong?
Your remarks on Kajimachi.
Kajimachi? Oh. You mean the fan that——
The Kinseki Group is the sole backer of our entire operation. They have a very tense relationship with Kajimachi right now—speaking out recklessly will only make things harder for all of us.
I don't understand what the big deal is. I was just thinking about how I shot my first movie there.
I still remember when I got started. You told me Chairman Tessai valued my skills...
The past is the past. I'm talking about the present. Things are different now.
Besides, we've always told you to stay in your lane and stick to the script when you're onstage. The people come to see Momoka, not you, with your big mouth.
I... I get it.
As long as you get it.
Producer, when are we going to the recording studio? My schedule is packed today. We have to get a move on.
Meh. Actually, why don't you take a few days off.
HUH? What?!
P-Producer! I don't need any rest! I'm used to the grind by now! We're in peak season. I know that. If I take a break now, that put us several days behind!
Maybe if you hadn't been so busy during the day yesterday, you would have been calmer last night.
I'm sorry, but——
If you really want to make up for your mistakes, Momoka, I have an urgent assignment for you.
Anything!
The television station wants to book you on a show. Take a look.
The Haunted House of Kajimachi?! Another one of these shows? Why?!
And why now, of all times, would they produce a program that makes Kajimachi look bad?
Then you'd rather take a break?
No! I just...
Momoka, you know that when you don't do what we say... it reflects... poorly on you.
But...
Momoka!
Producer, I don't want to die!
I've known that place was haunted for forever! A bunch of people died in that house!
They say it's... it's somehow connected to the 'Nameless Oni'. It was her there on the stage last night...
Momoka, I will take measures to ensure your safety.
(Quietly) Mio, back me up here.
Momoka, do not worry. I'll go with you.
Mio-san... is going too?
Yes.
Are you strong enough to take down that vicious Oni?
Mio tilts her head and thinks hard for a few seconds.
Maybe not.
I'm doomed. I'm done for!

lines.st: 'If we keep walking down Nita Street, we'll soon arrive at Kajimachi. From now on, that will be our place, our city.'
'Our city? But, Papa, we've been walking down the street, and I don't see anything all that different. What do you think, Uncle Tessai?'
'It will become different. It will change.'

lines.x: A wind carrying a scent—woody, or perhaps musty—blows against her face, and somehow, Hoshiguma is compelled to take a deep breath.
The houses are all so old.
Does Uncle Tessai still live in the same place?
Yeah. The big house at the end there.
Kinbee, what's the deal with the funeral? Kajimachi looks like it's doing fine?
It's a long story.
What's wrong?
Hoshiguma quickly realizes why Kinbee has fallen silent—and why the expression on his face now carries a touch more respect.
Not too far away, there's an old man standing in the road, facing them.
The old man's back is slightly hunched. Yet somehow, as the wind lifts his wide sleeves, he seems to stand a little taller.
Uncle Tessai!
Chairman Tessai.
Kinbee, I told you that you are not permitted to set foot in Kajimachi ever again.
The Phidia appears startled. The reverence on his face quickly turns to fear.
I was preoccupied with a certain 'happy event' that happened today. I lost track of my surroundings and accidentally walked into Kajimachi. Damn!
She's in cahoots with you. That's what you're happy about?
Chairman, I just got her out of the police station, is all. The cops started chasing her as soon as she arrived in Mitsukue. She only just slipped out.
She's come back here out of genuine concern for her home. If you're unhappy about something, please, take it up with me. Do not blame her!
As for my trespassing into Kajimachi—shall I'll perform a dogeza, offer a formal apology on another date, or cut off a finger—
Ha! You'd do any of those things if I asked?
The decision is yours—
*huff huff* Oyabun! I ran around to everyone asking where you were, until I found out you'd come here to Kajimachi!
Why did you make the trip out here? That old bastard doesn't have many days left—why bother coming to this forsaken place?
O-Oyabun?
You know I'm your oyabun?
Then do you know who gave this 'oyabun' the lofty position that he holds today?
I—
Ah, right, this isn't your first offense.
Some time ago, Chairman Tessai forbade me and my men from ever entering Kajimachi again. This is not the first time you've disobeyed that order. Am I right?
Well, I wasn't—

You were the one who drove those three families out of Kajimachi—plus the two shops that had to shut down, and that was all after Chairman Tessai ordered us to stay out of Kajimachi. Isn't that right?
You knowingly went against his orders. How should we punish you?
Beads of sweat form on the flustered gangster's forehead.
The oyabun isn't usually like this—he toasts me when I do well and offers gentle encouragement when I fall short.
Before he can even finish his thought, he hears a sound.
*swish*
Wait!
Kinbee, what are you doing?!
Before he can even finish his thought, it's cut short—the gangster's headless body collapses to the ground with a thud.
Neither Tessai's iron fan nor Hoshiguma's Hannya can match the quick draw of the Phidia's long sword.
That was your Kinsekikai brother!
Murdering a member of the family right in front of me. Is this your way of raising a hand against me?
But Chairman, you've already given me managerial control of the clan.
He ignored warnings, defied the chairman, and refused to change—he should've died long ago. The fact that he lived this long is a failure of my leadership.
Well, I brought her here; my work is done. It's time I answered for disobeying Chairman Tessai's orders.
He aims his bloodstained long sword—still unsheathed—at his finger.
Before the old man can even speak, the Phidia swings his blade, and his left pinkie falls to the floor.
There. That should atone for my sins, yeah?
No one says anything.
The Phidia slides the blade back into its sheath, bows deeply to Tessai, and gives a small nod to Hoshiguma.
Chairman Tessai, I will now take my leave, so as not to bother you further.
Welcome home, Hoshiguma.
I'm going to call the police—
Hoshiguma's voice trails off, as she becomes lost in thought, remembering how Kinbee swaggered around the police station.
The old man squints his eyes slightly, as he glances at Hoshiguma.
It seems you've been getting on well in Lungmen. You've forsaken the life of crime, eh?
If you still walked the path, you would remember this—no matter how a man dies, someone always comes to clean up.
And as long as the cleanup happens, the police won't bother themselves with our 'messes'.
You've had a long trip. Let's talk inside.
Though she has been away for twenty years, Hoshiguma still pushes open the gate with practiced ease.
Just like in Kajimachi, time has not left much of a mark on this place. Hoshiguma immediately spots the little path she used to take—the quickest way across the courtyard.
She can't tell whether the koi in the pond have lived all these years, or if the owner has been quietly replacing them with identical ones.
I'm back.
Sit.
Uncle Tessai, did you write me that letter because Kinbee—
I never wrote you a letter.
It wasn't you?
When you left Mitsukue twenty years ago, I told you we would never again make contact. I intended to honor that promise.
So, that letter...?
Kinbee forged it. What else could it be?
Why would he do that? You're the chairman of the Kinsekikai. How could you permit such a transgression?
Chairman? If that viper didn't keep calling me that, I'd have forgotten I was chairman of anything.
Look, I'm an old man with no real power. Even my last parcel of turf, Kajimachi, is about to be taken from me.
And Kinbee... heh, he's taken over the Originium crystal business that I built from the ground up. He's taken control of all our operations.
He's started new ventures like a TV network and even a talent agency, and made them hugely successful.
So he's usurped all Kinsekikai businesses and is now planning to take over Kajimachi?
'A radical rebirth and redevelopment plan' — that was how he put it, just a month ago.
Who's rebirthing what? How are they redeveloping it? Who keeps the proceeds? Ha. These are not questions I can ask, of course.
That's why you forbade him and his men from entering Kajimachi.
And today we get this grand performance, where he uses his minion's life as a stage prop… no, not a performance, it was a show of force.
He didn't use to be like this.
He was a better actor back then. He tricked me, and he tricked you. That's the only possible explanation, no?
You didn't realize that finger-chopping bit was all a ploy?
A... a ploy?
A fake finger? Surgical reattachment? A prosthesis? He's got no shortage of tricks.
He wasn't 'atoning for his sins'. He was spitting in my face! He was making a mockery of the Kinsekikai, of everything we stand for.
I don't know what scheme Kinbee had in bringing you back, but there's only one thing you should do now: return to Lungmen, and don't ever come back again. He's a rabid tuskbeast now.
But what will happen to you?
Me? As long as the Kinsekikai still exist, I'm still their chairman by title. Kinbee wouldn't dare raise a hand against me, at least not out in the open. And that's enough.
I can't just leave.
Then what do you intend to do? Wait until Kinbee has pushed me to the brink and then end my suffering?
I just don't want any more regrets this time.
So you regret going to Lungmen?
No. Everything I regret... is from my time in Mitsukue.
Watching Kinbee cut that man's head off like it was nothing... it sent all kinds of regret bubbling up from my stomach.
Kinbee's men see Kajimachi as nothing more than a pimple on Mitsukue's hide. You can't change their minds about that.
I'm going to do whatever I can.
By the way, amidst our grim conversation I almost forgot to ask... what happened with Hannya? When Kinbee was here, I took a glance at it. Looks different than it did the day I gave it to you, no?
Well, to be honest, a corner got sliced off.
How?
With a sword.
The old man's world-weary gaze suddenly flares with intensity.
Whose sword? What was it made of? How was their sword technique? Did they use any Arts?
A good friend of mine back in Lungmen, a colleague—
Explaining it all with words is going to take too long. Give Hannya to me. Let me see it.
Huh—tsk tsk...
A cut like this doesn't come from advanced reinforced steel or simple Arts alone. Sure, there's always someone stronger out there—but let's not forget, Hannya's no ordinary shield.
...Chi Xiao.
Uncle Tessai, you can tell just by looking at the cut?!
Don't forget, I've been around. Back when I was knocking back drinks with the Yanese trade envoys, the name 'Chi Xiao' came up more than once.
For Hannya, being bested by Chi Xiao is no shame at all.
At the end of the day, it wasn't sharpness or toughness that turned Chi Xiao and Hannya into legendary weapons.
Your mind was weaker than Chi Xiao's wielder. Not by much, but enough to turn the tide of battle.
In that case, I guess Kinbee's letter wasn't totally useless. At least it gave Hannya a chance.
Do you want to reforge it?
Of course. Do you expect me to let you run around Lungmen with this broken shield?
Still... I need to think for a bit about how I'll reforge this shield, to give you a sliver of a chance to best Chi Xiao. Let me think it over...
The old man, who once stared down disaster unblinking, now burns with the impatience of youth. He's learned that there's someone stronger out there, and he yearns to test his mettle.
The old acquaintance's long sword has just shattered any sort of 'parting sorrow', yet nostalgia stirs in Hoshiguma all the same.
Heh. Forget it. This isn't something you and I can figure out by just sitting here and thinking about it. Give me some time.
You've spent all night in an interrogation room. You must be tired. Go to the guest room and get some sleep.
No.
What?
I want to pay my respects to my father first.
I'm sorry.
He's your biological father. What are you apologizing to me for?
Is that clinic, my old home, still there?
It's there, but no one has lived in it for about a decade. I'd bet it doesn't much resemble its former self.
The abandoned house in the alley at the end of Kajimachi's North Street... everyone calls it the 'Haunted House'.
After the vicious Oni disappeared, Shimasu, who was the shateigashira, moved into the house with his family, but within three months, he fell ill and passed away.
Six months later, Sanoguchi, who was then serving as wakagashira-hosa, bought the house. After hiring an onmyoji to perform rites for seven days and nights, he moved in with his new bride.

On the seventh night, a patrolman heard wretched wails coming from the 'Haunted House'. In a fit of madness, Sanoguchi took up his family's heirloom blade, killed his wife, and then took his own life.
In the years that followed, every soul who deigned to live in the Haunted House met a terrible end. Eventually, no one dared go near it again.
Legend has it that the vicious Oni never really disappeared. From time to time, her tortured visage will pop up in the Haunted House.
And so, today...
It's too dark in here. I can't see anything...
(Lights lantern) M-Mio-san, are you there?
Whoa!
It's a pull-top!
Momoka, calm down. The camera on your head is shaking all over the place.
Where did Mio-san go? She said she'd be here with me.
She's feeling under the weather. She called in sick today.

I've known Mio-san for years, and I've never seen her get sick. Last winter, I watched her eat twenty ice creams with my own eyes, and she was completely fine. You're telling me she caught a cold? I don't believe it.
Momoka, we're just trying to add some color here for TV. Work with us, okay?
But I just saw that Oni at the stage, and now I'm going to visit her haunted house...
Ugh. Relax. You're going to be fine.
Alright... I'll do my best.
Ugh, why are there so many pull-tops here?
I don't like this... Wait! What's that sound behind me?
Who's there?
The vicious Oni? A ghost?!
It's you? What are you doing in my house?
Momoka grabs something shaped like a stick from the ground and points it at the shadowy figure.
But she quickly realizes that whatever she has picked up will be ineffective against ghosts or a fierce, vicious Oni. In a hurry, she shoves the stick-thing into her coat and pulls out a box...
...of roasted soybeans.
Vicious Oni! I-I've got roasted soybeans! If you dare come closer, I'll waste you!
Oni wa soto! Fuku wa uchi! Oni wa soto, fuku... fuku... err... uh...
The beans aren't working. But I have some salt here too! Check it out! Salt!
The thing... you... fear... most...
Well, I guess I'll be going. Sorry for intruding. Sorry for bothering you. Byebye. Sayounara!
Hey! Wait a second!
No, I don't think I will!
My head!
Are you alright?
Momoka, who has just struck her head on something, starts to fall to the ground. However, 'the ghost's' voice jolts her back on her feet.
I'm fine. I'm fine. Sorry for bothering you.
I've got uh, kids, and my parents, they're so old... to take care of back home!
No! Hasuke is at home, waiting for me to water him! Let me go! I have to go take care of him!
In her panic, Momoka lets out a high note she's never even hit in the recording studio.
Later that day, as the production team looks at the footage, they are unsure which one of the two, Momoka or Hoshiguma, most resembles a wailing wraith.
What... what is that thing at my feet?!
You're asking me?
No! I didn't ask you! Let me go!
The terrified girl suddenly disappears, kicking up a massive cloud of dust.
Perplexed, Hoshiguma leans in for a closer look, and finally sees what happened.
She smashed right through the floorboards.
Hello, madam. I don't think I've seen you in Kajimachi before. What's your name?
Do you need something? Some stranger ransacked my house. I need to go clean up.
You haven't heard the news? It seems that someone snuck across the northern border, into Mitsukue.
My name is Tetsuya Sorimachi. I'm a member of the Kinsekikai. Please tell me who you are. I know everyone here in Kajimachi, and I've never seen you here before.
Tetsuya? Could it be...?
No. No way.
I'm going home, Tetsuya.
Home? Don't be ridiculous. Do you even know the story behind this haunted house?
This was the home of the First Chairman of the Kinsekikai, and his clinic too. He was the best doctor we ever had. He saved countless lives.
After he passed away, the house went to his daughter.
Are you claiming to be his daughter?
Hold on... you're pretty tall, you have a single horn, and look at your hair... is that really you, Hoshiguma-aneki?
Aneki? Tetsuya, you—
—The Haunted House kaidan is about you?
I never died. I never disappeared. I never became a kaidan. I just went to Lungmen; that is all.
Who can verify the truth of what you're saying?
Go ask Uncle Tessai.
I have nothing to say to that man.
Oh? You're with Kinbee? Then go ask him about it. Don't bother me. I'm going to pay my respects to my father.
Come talk to Mifune-san with me. You can return home after we've sorted this out.
I politely refuse.
The young man takes out the wooden bokutou hanging from his waist and points it at Hoshiguma.
Upon seeing that his weapon is a blade made of wood, Hoshiguma lets out a sigh of relief. She sets down Hannya and faces Tetsuya, bare-handed.
Have we met?
Never.
Do you really think you can beat me with a bokutou?
I've heard the story of the 'Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai' before.
So you...
Tetsuya? You're picking another fight? Last time you provoked someone, it almost became an actual sword fight. Haven't you learned your lesson?
This is none of your business, Kichisei. Mifune told me to watch out for strangers. I need to take this woman to see him.
She doesn't look like an easy mark to me. Why are you——
Why are you picking fights with ghosts?!
You know her?
She popped up yesterday onstage during the concert and nearly scared Momoka half to death.
She's really a ghost?
No. What kind of ghost runs from, and then gets caught by, the cops? Did you somehow break out of jail, or were the cops unable to hold you?
They let me go, thank you very much.
Hmph. Boring.
Calm down, Tetsuya. Don't always take Mifune as the supreme authority.
I know you hate your father and don't wanna go home. But at least try to carry yourself with a little decency. Or else all Kajimachi is gonna treat you like the plague.
Kichisei!
So Uncle Tessai... remarried? And he gave you the same name as his first son?
He... treats you and your mother poorly?
Before, when we used to fight, I would ask him why he married my mother.

His first wife and son were killed, along with your father. Then the girl he had raised like his own daughter suddenly went missing. He was supposed to die alone, so why did he have to drag my mom into it? Why did he have to ruin our lives too?
Hoshiguma stares at the young man, who is staring back at her through his mask. She realizes he is not looking at her like an enemy...
*Sigh*
What are you sighing about?
Nothing. I'm just tired.
Don't lie to me!
The young man adopts a fighting stance and attempts to close in on Hoshiguma at a tricky angle.
Hey!
What kind of... empty-hand disarm technique is this?!
You read too many mangas. This is the most basic of basic grappling techniques.
Did you settle down yet?
The young man doesn't answer. Even though she's peering through a mask, Hoshiguma can clearly see that the young man looks a bit depressed.
You were all fired up just a moment ago. No need to give up so quickly. If your skills aren't up to par, just train harder.
Who says I'm giving up? How... how can you tell that I'm...?
Forget it.
So what really happened between you and Uncle Tessai?
You can't ask me that question, Hoshiguma-aneki.
The young man hooks the bokutou back at his waist. He wants to wave goodbye, but can't bring himself to do so.
After standing there for a moment, he gives Hoshiguma a stiff nod and limps off towards the outskirts of town.
Ahem, Ghost-san?
My name is Hoshiguma.
Hoshiguma, Tetsuya may be stupid, but he's not evil. He only started working for Mifune because he had a falling out with Old Man Tessai.

Lately, rumors have been flying about someone from the Northern Court sneaking into Mitsukue. Mifune probably decided he was too annoying and sent him to wander the city, telling him to go catch the northern spy, and he actually took it seriously.
This morning, Kinbee killed one of his minions here in Kajimachi, for the crime of trespassing.
Tetsuya is Uncle Tessai's son. I hope Kinbee doesn't go lop his head off too.
You look awfully young, Kichisei. What brings you to Kajimachi?
I inherited my grandmother's shop, the Lucky Star. Old Man Tessai's pachinko parlor is under it, so I help him mind his shop too.
You can handle two jobs at the same time like that?
*Shrugs* Ever since that bastard Mifune announced he was redeveloping Kajimachi, no one really comes by anymore. So I have plenty of free time.
What do the residents think of the redevelopment plan?
Huh? Mifune doesn't give two shits what the locals think.

His people don't usually mind 'trespassing into Kajimachi.' Some people come to stir up trouble, while others come to drive people out. We're powerless to stop them. Even Old Man Tessai can't keep them in check.
The Old Man's only been able to hold them off because he's got the deed to Kajimachi and is still the titular head of the Kinsekikai. Otherwise, Mifune would've taken it long ago.
Thanks for sharing all that.
No need to thank me. If you do end up moving back here, we'll be neighbors. Good neighbors help each other.
By the way, what were you calling Mifune... Kinbee?
That was his name before he hit it big.
Oh. Okay. Then that's what I'll call him from now on.
Oh, but, Ghost-sa——err... Hoshiguma-san, I really want to know more about you. Is the kaidan legend true? Did you really hunt and kill on a mission of vengeance twenty years ago?
Sorry, I don't have time to talk about that right now. I got a lot to do.
Sure, whatever. You go do your thing. I'll be right there in the pachinko parlor. Come find me if you have time.
Oh, by the way. I'm heading to the shrine to pray for Momoka tomorrow, and then there's the fireworks festival the day after that. So don't come either of those two days, since I'll be out!
Fireworks festival?
I guess... it is fireworks season.
Momoka sits in a cramped little room, in front of a plant that doesn't look particularly healthy.
It looks like a few more of your leaves have gone yellow. Well, at least you have a few new leaves to balance those out.
There's not enough sunlight in this room. Your stem keeps bending towards the window... ugh.
Please don't die, Hasuke.
Next to the plant, there is a whole row of baffling objects, arranged from right to left.
An empty water tank, a few flower pots filled with soil but no plants, a climbing frame for cloudbeasts, and in the middle—a tiny electronic terminal, so small that its purpose is indiscernible.
And on the far left, there is a tightly sealed gym bag. There's no way to tell what's inside.
As Momoka reaches for the watering can, the phone rings.
Mio-san...?
Is this Momoka?
Producer?!
Momoka, the TV station's ratings are through the roof, all thanks to your visit to that haunted house. Why aren't you the least bit excited? Have you not watched it yet, or...?
I was scared of that ghost thing before I ever agreed to do it. I hope I never see it again. That ghost...
The ghost didn't touch you!
Every major station is broadcasting your new show right now, and the forums are full of discussion about the Oni—some have even created dedicated threads to revisit your past episodes!
I was actually worried about your career trajectory for a while, but now I can finally relax. You're practically guaranteed to become the Southern Court's Kaidan Queen.
But...
Alright, well. The advertisers are blowing up my phone with endorsement offers for you. I'd better take some calls!
Momoka, dejected, looks back at the room. She fixes her gaze on the television.
Kajimachi is haunted! There are narrow old alleys, rundown shopping streets, and streetlights no one's even bothered to repair...
Oh no, no...
(Presses channel button to change the channel)
The vicious Oni has returned, all because of the countless wrongful deaths in Kajimachi!
With no one daring to even set foot in Kajimachi, what future lies ahead for the neighborhood?
(Mashes channel button)
Popular entertainer Momoka faces life-threatening danger in Kajimachi. Local businessman Mifune-san speaks out——
'On behalf of the Kinseki Group, I vow that we will fix Kajimachi's problems at their core.'
Why did it blow up like this? Usually people watch the show, laugh a little, and move on.
Why did this episode provoke such a reaction? I promised Kichisei I'd do my part for Kajimachi, but if things keep going like this…
What do I do now...?
Momoka takes a look at the forums. She doesn't even need to scroll to see her name. Every thread is about her.
No. I'm the cause of all this. I need to respond positively...
Will you support me, Hasuke?
Hasuke says nothing.

lines.st: Hello everyone. I am Momoka Hanyuu.
I have an announcement to make... regarding that TV show I did in Kajimachi.
You all know that I filmed my first movie in Kajimachi.
There was a time when lot of the people who lived there... walked the path of the yakuza... so disputes break out from time to time.
Now there are fewer and fewer people there. There's just a few quiet old streets. It's not a scary place at all...

lines.x: ...and... post!
(Deep breath)
Interacting with fans is usually so easy. Why am I so nervous this time...?
I hope I can help Kichisei and Kajimachi... hehe.
Let's just put the Kaidan Queen stuff aside for now.
Look at how many likes it's getting. Hehe...
Huh? What happened? Why did it get grayed out?
The phone rings again.
Who could it be...?
She runs to the phone and picks up the receiver.
Hello? Momoka?
It's clearly Mio's voice on the other end. Mio, who is usually cold, sounds like she's struggling to keep her emotions in check.
Before Momoka can even respond, someone else comes on the line.
Aho ka! Stupid girl, have you lost your mind? What are you doing posting like that?
We've already locked the post. It looks bad for us to delete it here from the system. You have to delete it yourself, now!
*Huff* That's... all I have for now.
'All you have'? It's a giant mountain!
If I'd shown up a little earlier and filmed you cleaning out the Haunted House and posted it to the intercity net… who knows, maybe it would've gone viral. Could've made us a bit of cash.
Now? My shift's almost over, and you bring a heaping pile of garbage over... *sigh*
This is just the trash from the first floor and the courtyard. There's more on the second floor and in the basement.
Uh oh.
Anyway, please take my money. Otsukaresama desu.
Alright, fine... Wait. A koban coin?
You don't use kobans anymore? Or you can't make change?
How long have you been away? We switched to paper currency ages ago. Actually, I've heard that Mitsukue and Kusarikawa want to eliminate cash all together.
That koban must be worth around four thousand mon. I can't make change for it, and even if I could, I wouldn't dare accept it as payment. Who knows if it's even real?
Uh...
Here's your money.
Woah, Tessai Oyabun!
Arigatou gozaimasu. I'll have someone haul this away then.
The recycler pulls out his terminal and begins dispatching vehicles and personnel, while Tessai and Hoshiguma remain standing by the trash heap. Neither of them appears ready to leave.
The evening air is thick and humid. As Hoshiguma looks at the trash heap, her thoughts return to Lungmen.
If it was Missy standing here with me, she would've already dragged me back to her place by now, she thinks.
And if it was Ch'en Hui-chieh, she would've rolled up her sleeves, without saying a word, and started hauling trash from the basement and second floor.
If it was 'Lin Gray' or his daughter... I mean, they would never have even let the house become haunted in the first place.
The old man beside her remains silent. The garbage truck arrives, gets loaded up with trash, and then rumbles away.
Did you find any of your father's things while cleaning out the house?
I searched the whole first floor but didn't find anything.
Uncle Tessai, I just ran into a young man named Tetsuya—
Come stay with me. This place is too messy. It'll take you days to clean it up.
That's okay, I'd rather stay here in my own home.
Fair enough. I'll go and get you some clean sheets.
Otsukare, Uncle Tessai.
As Tessai slowly walks away, Hoshiguma turns her gaze to the empty backyard.
When her father died, Mitsukue still hadn't completely recovered from the chaos of the Bloodpeak Campaign.

In accordance with the old customs, she had her father cremated. She buried his ashes in the backyard. Also, she erected a marker, made of the finest wood, and implored Tessai to carve the inscription.

When she came to clean out the backyard, all these years later, she found the wooden marker was still standing firm, even amidst all the garbage. She's been so busy cleaning up that she hasn't had time to tend to the grave yet.
Ao-Oni-san? You're Ao-Oni-san, aren't you?!
Hey, everybody! Come over here! We have to get footage of this! It's a huge discovery! The Ao-Oni from the kaidan is here in Kajimachi!
We come to you this evening from Kajimachi, Mitsukue with an amazing discovery——the Ao-Oni!
After twenty years, the legendary Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai, has returned! We caught a glimpse of her yesterday at the concert on Nita Street, and now she stands here before us!
Is she a master mimic? A ghost wandering this mortal coil? A cold-blooded killer who's been silently stalking Mitsukue for twenty years, leaving a trail of bodies in her wake?
She doesn't look like a ghost... Ghosts don't usually have physical bodies... or cast shadows! Look. The Ao-Oni standing before us is casting a shadow! Hello! I'm Komatsu, from Kinseki Channel.
Hmph.
Where's your unicycle?
My what?
Many people think the Ao-Oni is somehow related to the Wanyuudou! A bunch of the Oni's victims met... considerable misfortune right after spotting that fiery wheel—I think...
Before the reporter finishes speaking, Hoshiguma turns around and begins walking toward the house, only to bump into another hurried reporter.

Ao-Oni-san! I just want to ask you a few questions—
Gugh—

Tessai?!

I've got your sheets here, and your drink.
Drink?

Arigato. Will you come with me, Uncle Tessai?

Hmm...
Who's there?!
Is that a car horn?
(Rubs eyes) Am I dreaming? Still asleep?
Under the bright moonlight, there's a unicycle. With all of the garbage cleared away, it stands there in clear view.

Did I have too much to drink? How can that be? I only had half a bottle of sake.
Stranger still, she sees a tiny helmet suspended in the air above the unicycle. If an invisible person were riding that unicycle, the helmet looks like it would be the perfect size for them.
But since Hoshiguma sees no invisible rider, to her eyes, the little helmet is simply… floating in midair.
Is it a bug planted by the police, or one of Kinbee's tricks? Is it Uncle Tessai's doing? Was it created with some Arts?
Many people think the Ao-Oni is somehow related to the Wanyuudou! A bunch of the Oni's victims met... considerable misfortune right after spotting that fiery wheel—I think...
The Wanyuudou? The kaidan is real?
Well, in any case, I'd better go take a look——
As Hoshiguma bends down to examine it, the unicycle's pedals start spinning, and the unicycle, with its helmet, shoots off.
Wait... stop right there!
The unicycle speeds ahead, with Hoshiguma chasing it.
Hoshiguma can clearly see that the spinning pedals are propelling the unicycle forward and the tiny helmet is moving up and down in rhythm...
Why does it seem like an invisible person is riding the unicycle? When the vehicle turns, its pedals scrape the ground, causing a few sparks.
Momoka's body goes limp as she falls onto her tatami mat.
I can't sleep a wink.
Maybe I should go to the agency tomorrow morning...
First, I need to apologize properly, and really mean it… If that's not enough, I can take on some extra work. Then Mio-san will forgive me—
Aahh!
Is that... a clock?

An advertisement, loud, exaggerated, and contrived, yet as reassuring as Mitsukue itself, begins to sound.

...peace of mind and financial security, Mitsukue Life Insurance Company.
(Music)
Konbanwa. This is the Kinseki Channel Nightly News. The time is now 1:30 AM.
It's almost the witching hour... when the ghosts are most active... ugh...
...the plan formed with the Northern Court was thwarted...
...Heavy rain beats down on Isazaki; damage to the agricultural plate remains under control...
...now let's turn to the Mitsukue entertainment industry, which is turning some heads...
(Shrill alarm)
What? What happened? An earthquake? A Catastrophe?
(Sound of signal interference)
—Emergency. Emergency. This is not a drill. This is not a drill.
We are under attack by the Northern Court. Repeat, we are under attack by the Northern Court.
For your own safety, please follow these instructions:
(Unknown, ear-piercing sound)
The Northern Court? How can that be?! What's really going on here?
(Sound of signal interference)
Momoka! Momoka! Momoka!
What's going on? That's... a recording of my concert?
Alone in this land, we all float on by♪
Our fleeting tender moments, cause such deep sighs♪
Let me forever gaze into your...♪
(Audio glitches)
...gaze into your—gaze into your—gaze into your—gaze into your—gaze into your—gaze into your—

—If you are inside, close all doors and windows—
Gaze into your——
Gaze into your——
AHHH! AHHHHHHHHH!!!
Momoka jumps up from the tatami with a start. The thing that has just appeared on the screen is simply too bizarre. She flings whatever is in her hands at the TV in a panic.
She hasn't touched the remote at all, but the TV starts to flick through the channels by itself.
...and now, finally, we welcome you all to the 'Midnight Heartbeat Cloudbeast Bar' on Nita Street —
Mifune~ ♪ Credit Bank~ ♪
Mi...Mif...Credit...
Chairman... give her another chance.
That voice...?
A familiar female voice emanates from the TV. The fear in Momoka's heart slowly eases, and she peeks out from behind the giant pillow in an attempt to see the screen more clearly.
We are at a crucial crossroads.
I understand. However... we do not have evidence of her meeting Tessai in private.
Mio-san? And Mifune, the chairman of the group?!
Why is it playing on the TV? A recording, or a livestream?
Momoka rubs her eyes.
Mio, she's out of control.
Even if this wasn't on Tessai's orders, if it happened once, it'll happen again. So do what you did before—make her vanish quietly, without a trace.
Mifune-san, now is not the right time.
You're still pleading for mercy on her behalf.

Must you always wear that pitiful expression?
Since you have a soft spot for her, fine, just find any excuse to terminate the contract and force her into retirement... Oh, right, and find a way to get her infected with Oripathy.
Mifune-san, I——
You have two paths to choose from. Take your pick.
Don't forget—out of a house full of trash, she just had to pick up that broken blade. We searched everywhere and couldn't find it, so we had no choice but to hastily forge a fake one.

Right...
Oripathy... Mifune-san... is telling Mio-san to...

After falling out of the Haunted House, Momoka quickly changed outfits, wrapping her dirty clothes into a bundle, and left the filming location.
The 'stick' was most likely wrapped up with the bundle of dirty clothes.
When Momoka stands up and goes to the entrance, she takes the stick-like thing out of her dirty clothes.

'...changed everything'? This broken blade changed everything?

She turns the desk lamp on and off... and then on again... and off again.
With the light on, she's afraid someone will see her shadow in the window.
With the light off, she's afraid some intruder will burst in, under cover of darkness.
They're talking about how they'll get rid of me? Just because of what I posted online? Just because I picked up a broken blade?
Am I gonna get disappeared? Infected with Oripathy?
No way. I must have it wrong. They didn't even mention my name—
Suddenly, she hears footsteps coming from downstairs. They sound familiar, steady, and fast.

...All the way to the window
...Momoka?
I figured you were sleeping, so I just let myself in.

The one you're going to... dispose of, it's not me, is it?
Dispose of you? Momoka, you—

I'm going to help you.

I'm going to help you.

Momoka takes a few breaths, and to her amazement, finds herself...

It's not a normal unicycle, it's a small—

Are you sure you're chasing 'a unicycle with a small helmet floating midair above it'?

An information broker.

Deal?
Deal.

EEK! WHY ARE YOU HERE?!

Omae! Don't think you're getting away!

OnlywhenfilminganditwasfakeIDON'THAVEACHOICEANYWAYS!

C-Countersteer, then lean in...

Kinbee?!

I'm so tired. I wanna sleep.
Locking in for two hours straight is my limit. I'm so sleepy.

I have to go back to that cramped and lousy office—

Itadakimasu.

The 'Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai', the 'Single-horned Oni', among many more nicknames.

A tantou.

Kinbee?
Mifune.

The Ao-Oni?!

Oh yeah.

The sky gradually brightens.
Hoshiguma-san, the place is right up ahead.
Alright—wait, you should slow down—we're gonna hit the gate at this rate!
Huh?!

Kannushi-sama! A woman's fighting her way in again! She's on a bike this time!
Again?!

'Them'?
Check and see if it's a Vulpo, won't you?
Er...
A Vulpo? No, it's an Ægir and an Oni.
They brought back terrible memories of last time, and I didn't look at them clearly in my panic, sorry...
It's not your fault that concern wrecked your nerves.
Whoever our guests may be, I'll simply entertain them. The rest of you, calm down and focus on your own work.
I'm so sorry! I didn't mean to do this! I was just so nervous I forgot to use the brake—
I was wondering who it was. Are you not Momoka-san?
K-Kannushi-sama...
Uh, you know each other?
Momoka-san used to be a regular visitor here. She last came five years ago, and I thought perhaps her wishes came true and that she was now living a happy life.
No, not really... I just got so busy that I no longer had the time to visit.
No need to be sad. This is the Kizuna Jinja after all. You found success in your career, so I'm sure you formed lots of ties and connections with others.
*Cries*...
May I know how to call Momoka's friend here?
I'm Hoshiguma. Looking for someone.
Whom, may I ask? The shrine is quite bereft of clergy.
No, I'm not looking for a priest. I came here to find a 'kami-sama' for somebody.
Specifically, I'm looking for a unicycle with an invisible rider. I guess people in Mitsukue call it 'Wanyuudou'. Is it really a kami?
If they were looking to establish some sort of connection or looking for a blessing, I'm afraid they should come here themselves. Prayers made on someone else's behalf will not work.
He just wants to meet the kami. Didn't seem to care much about the forming ties or getting blessed type of thing.
Moriuchi-kun is truly a stubborn one... No, I should praise him for his tenacity.
You know him?
He's the one who insisted on meeting a specific kami with no want of blessing. We've become quite well-acquainted over the years.
So this means you can help him find the kami, right?
Nah, Moriuchi asking us to do it for him means the priest failed.
It is as you say. We priests cannot do anything if the kami themselves are unwilling to appear.
Well, the two of you haven't technically disrespected the shrine, other than driving your scooter onto our grounds.
Wh-What should we do? Do I have to apologize to the kami-sama? How do I do that?
Let me think... To appease the kami's anger, please go over to the omamori stall there and buy a full set.
What?

Then go to the hundred-mon omikuji and draw fortunes until you get one each of 'sue-kichi', 'chu-kichi', 'dai-kichi'. It's alright if you draw any of the others—unless you get 'dai-kyou'—then you'll have to start over.
Eh?!

—Finally...
I'm just joking. The kami would never punish someone over a scooter.
Wha—?!
I pray the two of you will obtain what you truly desire here. Now, plenty of divine work awaits me, so I shall take my leave.
Phew! I was worried I wouldn't have enough mon for all that!
Seems like the kannushi isn't going to get in our way, so things are going pretty well for us.
Let's take it easy. Why not go over to the omikuji section for a break, while I look around the place?
Um... that works.
Hoshiguma slowly goes around the vacant shrine grounds.
She knows so little about shrines and Shinto she cannot even tell if it's a 'honden', 'haiden', or 'heiden' standing before her.
The only things resembling Moriuchi's keychain are the two statues behind the stone lanterns that were obviously based on Perro Beast Stewards.
But the breeze here is cool, and she cannot help but to recall her youth when she visited a monastery with her father.
Her father and Tessai's figures appear and disappear between the pine and cypress trees, and the abbot pats her head and she hears the tale of the first Oni.
That Oni's name seems to be... 'Qui'lon'...
Hoshiguma-san, Hoshiguma-san!
What's up?
It's bad!
You got a 'dai-kyou'?
No, a 'sue-kichi'—no, I didn't mean that! I mean, I heard something!
The Wanyuudou?
I don't know! Maybe, but what if... it's a ghost...?
A ghost showing up this early in the morning might be some sort of super scary evil spirits, like onryo!
We should trust the kannushi a little. You think he'd let evil spirits in here?
True, but—
There's that sound again!
Calm down.
Ugh, it's not like I could do that on command, even if I wanted to...
I didn't expect It'd actually show up here. There's tracks on the ground...
The unicycle seems to realize Its tracks are giving It away, and turns toward the stone pavement.
Wh-What do we do?
Isn't this better for us? Now It's locked onto the stone pavement.
But... how is a kami unable to hide Its own tire tracks...?
What... do you mean... unable!
I-It spoke?!
Behind the Beast Steward statue! Momoka, you're closer to It, over there!
I don't see It? Where did It go?
No, It circled around to your back—
My... back?
Eek! A g-g-g-ghost's after me?!
Misfortune befalls anyone who sees the Wanyuudou, but It's actually chasing me—is my life over?!
Instantly, the girl and unicycle seem to change positions, the latter now hot on Momoka's heels, as if she was being chased by It from the very start.
Momoka, hold still!
S-STILL?!
She does not grasp what is happening, but still subconsciously stops her steps.
Hoshiguma snatches the chance to hurl Hannya out, and the giant triangular shield blocks the unicycle's path, forcing It to brake.
Now keep holding position, Momoka! I'll catch It. Don't, and I mean it, don't panic if It runs toward you!
O-O-Okay!
Hoshiguma closes in on the unicycle, blocking It from any easy escape routes.
It stops in Its tracks as the handlebars twist left and right—perhaps It does not know where to go, although it is no difficult decision.
On one side is a tall Oni, while on the other stands an Ægir It has just chased all over the place...
The pedals spin frantically, and the unicycle speeds straight for Momoka.
Momoka, grab It!
The unicycle dashes toward her, and Momoka's instinct screams at her to turn and run. But she keeps it a thought and does not move her legs.
Yabai. There's no other way, it's over, it's all over—such thoughts flood her mind as she stands there like a fowlbeast in headlights.
The unicycle approaches.
According to the kaidan tales, she is doomed—wait, that's not right. Wasn't she already down on her luck before running into the Wanyuudou?
Or maybe, if she hadn't run into Hoshiguma at the oden yatai, she would long since—
Momoka gulps, looks at Hoshiguma, then at the unicycle only a few steps away from her—
Instead of retreating, she jumps forward.
Kannushi-sama, someone's disgraced the shrine by... jumping... into the pond...
Momoka? Hoshiguma too?
I don't know what's going on, but Ill help you two out! Forgive me, Kannushi-sama!
Hoshiguma? Kichisei?
And...
One side of the pond lies a small unicycle leaning against a rock, pedals still moving on their own.
The thing on the unicycle appears before her, with a cycling helmet fitted perfectly on Its head. It's bright round eyes stare somewhat helplessly at all the water dripping to the floor.
Beside her, Hoshiguma's expression softens after making sure she's alright.
She nods in greeting to the person behind her, who Momoka guesses is Kichisei, then turns to look into the distance...
No matter what happened, it seems like she's finally accomplished something?
So, in the end... It was a ghost after all?
Have you ever seen a ghost that could be struck by water, girl?
Besides, it was just a round of hide-and-seek. Why were you going at it so seriously?
S-Seriously?
You looked like your life was on the line when you went into the pond with me.
Same goes for Moriuchi too. A kami I may be, but what makes him so sure a random god he finds on the streets is bound to be his maneki-neko?
Momoka, do you want me to lure the priest away? Or—
No, it's alright. The person we're looking for—no, the 'kami' we're looking for is right here.
Relax, I'm already right before your eyes. No more running for me.
I'll watch over you, and no one on the shrine grounds will cause you any trouble. Soak in the pond for as long as you like.
Momoka carefully places a hand on the kami, and this time It no longer dodges her. Although It fidgets to her touch, but It seems to at least hold no objections to mortal contact.
The cool breeze drags slowly across the pond, as if all that running and chasing that happened before dawn was merely a strange and messy, but not terrifying dream.
Empty handed, are you?
You couldn't even catch a girl scared out of her wits at the mere mention of a kaidan?
Silent, are you? We're all family here, we can laugh all this off, yes?
That's not it, Oyabun!
She was with that Ao-Oni, and we weren't strong enough...
Weren't strong enough?
Mifune takes a fierce step forward, face nearly coming into contact with the bold yakuza.
Colluding with Tessai to obstruct Kajimachi's development, disobeying my orders, and fleeing without permission. Even, getting mixed up with that individual...
Do you think you could get off just saying you weren't strong enough?
Oyabun, I... I...
Do you know what happened to Takehiko?
Y-Yes. You were forced to kill him with your own hands, and afterwards organized a grand funeral for him, even weeping as you held a memorial at his grave...
And who truly killed him? Was it me?
Of course it was that old bastard Tessai, he—
It was you! All of you! One by one, you proved your incompetence, allowing Tessai to get off scot-free, and that's how Takehiko died!
Mifune's hand slowly reaches inside his sleeve, as if to take something out.
The gangsters dare not move until their Oyabun pulls his fist back out into the open, slowly opening it...
Several heavy gold coins fall to the ground.
Are those k-koban coins?
I found these in a drawer. Pick them up. They're yours now.
Arigatou, Oyabun! But what for...?
The next time you would tell me you're not strong enough, just cut off your own arm and use these to buy you a state-of-the-art prosthetic. Am I clear?
Oyabun.
Speak.
Our people went northward from Nita Street and located Momoka and Hoshiguma... inside the shrine.
Do you hear that? The ninja I personally cultivated are more capable than you lot. Learn from them, all of you.
The shrine, eh?
Yes.
A shrine. Sacred ground, unsullied land. No one will be happy if trouble occurs there.
You mean...?
Take this useless bunch and surround the place. But be discreet—just make sure there is no way out.
If they intend to hole up in there, send a few men in to force them outside. Don't let anyone they've had contact with off either. If you can't make a move near the shrine, do it elsewhere.
Then the clergy inside...
The clergy? We can't touch them yet.
Understood.
Go. I have guests to see.
Mifune-san...
The frost on Mifune's face does not disappear at once, but it slowly melts when he sees Tetsuya, as if somewhat comforted by a hope for a new life after much toil and tumult.
The guys who just left...
Same slackers as always. You're the hardest worker I've got.
Enough about them. How was your visit home?
The Old Man... is the same as he ever was.
Don't feel bad.
I remember where you were only this tall, standing in-between your parents as they linked hands with you...
*Sighs* Who would have ever thought that your father would have no choice but to let go of that which was dearest to him, all for the Kinsekikai's sake...
Mifune-san, there's no need to compliment my father. Being chairman for too long has robbed him of all human emotion.
That can't be. The bond between parent and child is stronger than any other tie.
I used to treat your father as my own, in my younger days. Unfortunately the Old Man scorned me, and that's how our hostilities began.
But this Northern Court spy incident may prove to be an opportunity.
You mean...?
Your mother was wrongly accused because of her ambiguous relationship with an individual in the Northern Court. If you catch the spy, your father's attitude will surely improve.
And it just so happens I have the latest intelligence in my hands.
Tell me, please! No matter where the spy is, I'll root him out!
This spy... is in Kajimachi, and is highly likely to be related to the so-called Haunted House.
No way... Hoshiguma-san?
She—no, she has nothing to do with this, only returned here by chance.
But I can't get in there, if she stops me...
Simply go in when she's away. Kajimachi is a big town after all, and finding any additional clues will be good. Go!
A-At once!
Mifune's expression changes again when he sees a woman enter the room after Tetsuya leaves.
Tired, weary, impatient, and a sort of confidence mixed within. A sort of certainty that everything is under control and he merely has to wait for an opportunity to strike.
Mifune-san!
How are you doing with that task I gave you?
Our Diet Member has finally approved the separation of the Kinseki Group from the Kinsekikai.
As long as you now accept the role of chairman, the Kinseki Group and the Kinsekikai can go their separate ways.
Then why don't we hold the succession ceremony during tomorrow's fireworks festival? What do you think?
Tessai's views have not changed. Now, he even has Hoshiguma on his side, is one day really enough time for us to...?
Yes.
I saw your brow twitch. I know what you're thinking. You know I don't joke about things like this, yet you still don't believe me. Basically, you think that...
I shouldn't have forced you to forge a letter in the Old Man's name, and that calling Hoshiguma back here did nothing but cause trouble for everyone... is that it?
Twenty years ago... Hoshiguma... and that shield of hers... spilled quite a bit of blood. After all, it was the last thing the Old Man forged... Mio, have you ever been to his home?
Yes, many years ago.
What is his house full of?
Televisions.
That's right, televisions.
The Old Man might be stubborn, but his instincts are still sharp, some of the time.
He knew that if the Kinsekikai continued their unending torrent of bloodshed, we were bound to be destroyed sooner or later. So he set his sights on Originium crystalline components.

He quickly converted his metalworking shop into a workshop for crystalline components, and it was an instant success. After that, the Old Man no longer had time to forge the legendary weapons he loved most.
Just as his crystal business was really taking off, the Old Man suddenly acquired the materials, and techniques, to forge shields from a fallen noble house of the Mitsumoto faction.
They say that he spent nearly half of the Group's liquid assets to acquire them.
That shield, the last shield the old man ever forged, the shield he had spent so many assets to acquire... do you ever think it might have had some special significance to the Kinsekikai?
The transition to a new leader.
Even you knew that... but the Old Man just let the matter sit there. He kept putting it off and putting it off, all the way until the Southern Court was about to hold the first meeting of its Diet.
