She casts her gaze toward the tall walls in the distance. There are lights flashing outside, and the sound of tracks rolling over the ground.
She does not care what it means. Kajimachi is dead, and she cares not what will become of it later.
All she knows is that she must stay with the dying man.
The old man's breathing grows weaker and weaker, but he begins to feel the rumbling of tracks too.
He looks up in terror, but his eyes can no longer see. The ground trembles beneath him.
Is that... a sound... I can't hear...?
What is it, Hoshiguma... what is it...?
It's—
Hoshiguma stops in mid-sentence, choking back tears.
After a while, she growls from the back of her throat—
It's the signal... to begin the fireworks show.
The fireworks... have begun?
Yes. They're getting ready. You hear the commotion, don't you?
Yes... Kajimachi hasn't been so lively... in a long time...
A smile appears on the old man's face.
An engineering vehicle rams through the manor's walls, causing the beams to shake violently.
Not too far away, the pachinko parlor's machines are crushed to pieces, sending tiny balls flying everywhere.
Do you hear... the fireworks...?
Yes...
The sound of fireworks going off, taiko drums rumbling, the noise of the crowd.
He feels the trembling of the ground again, its joyful dance.
He hears the faint sound of car horns, from vehicles caught in the traffic jam outside.
He senses his own heartbeat growing slower by the second.
No...
No... I can't...
In his final moments, his heart races once more, and hot blood flows swift through his veins, as the dying man gazes at the sunset, thinking that it is the rising sun—
The wrinkled hands fall weakly.
Found the body that the witness reported... and the suspect, matching the description of Hoshiguma!
Was this part of Kinbee's plan too?
Shut up and come with us.
Calling the police, letting you see me with Uncle Tessai as he draws his last breath, so that I'll once again be suspected of murder...
Was this part of Kinbee's plan too?
Did his people call you?
...I see.
Hoshiguma takes a leap and sees a familiar foe before the policeman says anything.
Yakuza. Ninja.
You're in league with each other?
The policeman looks down at his own feet—as Hoshiguma swings her unsheathed sword, trying to cut a path through the faint but inevitable dusk.
Don't just stand there! These scoundrels aren't half as well-behaved as the suspect!
...I'm tired.
You think I'm not?
I mean, I really am tired.
We and a whole bunch of scum couldn't catch one bloody woman. Kuso. Kuso.
She wouldn't touch a hair on our heads. The real scum are the ones she knocked out with her sheath, all of them have a rap sheet ten thousand ri long.
And we're working with these scum against her, while she knocks them out one-by-one...
Kuso... Kuso!
Koretou keeps saying, 'it'll get better', but I've been on the job for almost ten years, and I don't see things improving one bit—
Out of the way, cop!
Shougo!
How dare you attack a police!
I told you to get out of the way—
Why you—
They stare into each other's eyes.
Excuse me, officer. May I pass?
Or, I could commit assault on an officer of the law. Or murder. I'm not picky at the moment.
The officer sighs and stands aside, putting his empty hands where Hoshiguma can see them.
Thanks. For Shougo.
Ding, dong, ding, Lingones Department Store♪
Everything you want, you'll find it here, Lingones Department Store♪
Come in, have a pint! We have just the right lager for an Oni like you—
Or, if you prefer the more traditional stuff, we have a fine collection of imo shochu for you to pick from...
Take this, ojou-san.
Out of the way.
What's the hurry? Are you looking for the most precious thing on Terra, the stepping stone to 'happiness'—
Wait... you're the Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai?
May I ask where Momoka-san is?
Are you a fan? Or are you after Mifune's bounty?
Neither. I just want to ask a favor.
Can I ask you to 'disappear' me like Momoka-san? Maybe that'll make me go viral like her!
It's the Ao-Oni!
Can I get a pic?
Out of the way.
Wait, Ao-Oni-san—
Uh... Ao-Oni-san, we're just a drinking establishment...
One Moeshima Black, to go.
The Moeshima Black production line shut down a few years ago. May I interest you in the newest offering from the same maker? It's their premium line, which I'm sure you'll be happy with—
Fine. Whatever.
While the clerk gets the liquor, Hoshiguma stands with her back against the storefront, gazing coldly at the crowd that has begun to gather.
A city is a big place, whether it's called Lungmen or Mitsukue.
The death of one man does not move the heart of the whole city, much less the death of a yakuza figurehead.
One man has died. Many still live.
But the people who still live, this city that they live in, has nothing to do with her anymore.
No one is following.
This is a good place.
Hoshiguma opens the bottle, and the smell of alcohol gushes out.
Just smelling it is enough to make her light-headed.
This one's for you, Uncle Tessai.
Ugh... how could this stuff taste so thin, when it's so expensive and the alcohol content so high?
This is the second time I've drank to someone. The first was to my father at my old home.
The kaidan chasers must be thrilled about the Ao-Oni drinking with a dead man.
Kaidan... hah.
The story of me and Higashi ended when I chose to step up for what was right, when I really didn't know what it was.
It was stupid, even though I survived. Ch'en, too, faced the same choice, and she survived as well, but I still think it's stupid.
You should have met Ch'en Hui-chieh, Uncle Tessai.

She's a true kyou, although they pronounce it a different way in Yan. But it refers to the same thing: Someone who's willing to stand in the way of an army a thousand strong, armed with nothing more than a sword.
This one's to her.
But I'm not like her.
She's brave. Unlike me—
Hah, awkward, isn't it?
But I'm scared... I can't deny it.
I'm not worried about my own life. But what about other people? Those I'm trying to protect? What about...?
...What about my home?
Honestly, I should just set out for Lungmen right now, and never look back.
It's a big deal, an L.G.D. officer suspected of murder in Higashi. That'd make a headache for everybody, from Missy to Mr. Lin to Madam Fumizuki to Mr. Wei.
They won't abandon me. I know what kind of people they are, and they know me well.
But I caused this.
Will my new home suffer for the trouble that I caused?
How could I keep calling it home, in that case?
Or I could draw my sword and become a real Oni. A killer.
Kill Kinbee, kill any of his men who dare draw on me, kill any police who stand in my way...
Turn into a nightmare that roams the streets of Mitsukue, become a true monster, become the kaidan after all.
Take vengeance. Go with the tide, embrace the times, become the maddest of the revelers, and become exactly what he...
No, exactly what everyone in Mitsukue wants me to be.
There's no way out. Every path leads to a dead end.
Is the bottle empty already?
You certainly can hold your drink, Hoshiguma-san.
Who's there?!
Where are your men?
I came alone.
Where's Uncle Tessai's body?
In police possession. Cause of death was a sword wound, but it was not your sword that did the deed.
Then why are your men after me?
Orders from above.
Are you here to arrest me too?
No, I'm here to drink with you.
Without waiting for an answer, the policeman sits down on the ground and takes out a small glass from his pocket.
Damn, this stuff is strong.
You want to try to convince me to come quietly, so you can carry out those orders from above?
Then, get you out of Mitsukue and back to Lungmen.
Against the orders of your superiors?
Why do you think I had you taken into custody the moment you entered Mitsukue?
I would have done the same, if a gang leader who had committed a major crime in Lungmen twenty years ago came back.
Is that all?
What else could it be?
The policeman looks straight into Hoshiguma's eyes.
When he realizes that Hoshiguma is not joking or hiding anything, he suddenly punches her in the face.
I can't believe you forgot.
The Diet candidate killed by the Kinsekikai was named Koretou.
That was for my uncle.
Kono bakayaro. His murder would not have gone ignored all these years, if you didn't stupidly claim responsibility for your stupid yakuza honor.
Sorry...
It's a little too late for that. There's nothing I can do about Mifune now.
He's in cahoots with one of the most powerful Diet member. How else could a yakuza have gained so much influence over the police?
The best I can do—the best the police can do is to let you go, so he won't get what he wants.

And Uncle Tessai's death will be nothing more than a footnote in the Ao-Oni kaidan legend and the Haunted House, and nothing will ever get resolved. Same as it was back then, am I right, Detective Koretou of the Spiritual Affairs Station?
You say you don't want him to get what he wants, but all you're doing is helping him.
You think Mifune is behind the kaidan.
You don't sound surprised.
A cop is a cop, even if he's from the Spiritual Affairs Station. I can smell where the rot is coming from, even with this chained collar around my neck.
So you've made peace with the chains?
All you need to worry about is filling your own stomach with fried meatchop with rice. Not all of us are the same.
Do you know how much it costs to get a house that can barely squeeze a family of four in Mitsukue?
I'm not sure I want to know.
If you're willing to pay off my loans and take my wife and kids to Lungmen, sure, I'll turn your old home upside down.
Or, if you're absolutely certain that it'll be a fatal blow to Mifune, I could potentially see myself jumping on this gamble of yours.
Now, it's your turn to answer.
Do you plan on paying off these loans that I'm not even sure I could free myself of before I die, or is there a greater power than the Diet, enough to take Mifune out once and for all?
I'm doing my best to help you. Recognize good faith when you see it, Ao-Oni.
The policeman angrily downs the last bit of liquor left in the cup.
Come with me.
No.
Give me a reason.

lines.st: I want my sacrifice to mean something, but everything in Mitsukue is denying it.
What else is left for me in this empty city?
It's time to go.
It's time to go home.
I hope Missy had a better summer in Siesta than I did here—

lines.x: ...guma!
Who's there?
Who's what? What are you talking about?
...Hoshiguma!
Ghost-san!
Momoka?!
Do you hear Momoka's voice, Koretou?
Momoka? The idol? Isn't she supposed to be with you? I was going to ask where you hid—
I'm over here!
This way!
Hoshiguma runs in the direction of the voice.
Koretou sighs and follows her.
There's no one here. But the voice is getting closer—
Over here! This way!
Momoka?!
What—
...What are you doing in a TV?
Neither Hoshiguma nor Koretou say it out loud.
A shabby TV set lies outside a tiny shop. It is hard to tell what the shop does, or if it is even open at all.
Inside, Momoka is yelling something at Hoshiguma.
She is not looking at Hoshiguma, but knocking on something in front of her, as though she is trying to break the TV screen and climb out.
You're there, right, Hoshiguma?
I'm here! Can't you see me?
I can't see you, but I can hear you!
Oh, I'm so relieved... I thought it was all over...
Hold the tears. Where are you? What's going on with this TV?
Kichisei and I are being held prisoner at Kinbee's base—
Bzzt.
Momoka?!
The TV goes dark.
A cold wind blows through the gaps between the buildings, and Hoshiguma's sword hums in its sheath.
Koretou.
I was agitated by Uncle Tessai's death and forgot a few things. My bad.
But what about you? Did you really think I'd hidden Momoka?
Kichisei too. I guess she's not important to you, nothing more than an inveterate pachinko gambler.
Hoshiguma—
What's one or two innocent lives to you, as long as you get this troublemaker out of town? After all, they're already knee-deep in this Kinbee mess. Why bother?
We'll do our best to protect them—
The people who died in the Haunted House, Uncle Tessai, that yakuza killed in Kajimachi a few days ago... since when have you been any good at protecting anybody?
It is Hoshiguma's turn to punch the detective in the face.
The detective stumbles back a few steps before steadying himself.
I don't need you to remind me of my failures.
But you're not a troublemaker to me. You're my trump card, the only thing that can hurt Mifune, the only thing standing between him and total control of Mitsukue.
And the way you play it is to get me out of the city?
It's the best a cop can do.
A cop, eh?
You're a cop too. You know the limitations.
Go back to Lungmen, Hoshiguma. Your 'particular individual' and the L.G.D. will take care of things for you.
Hoshiguma instinctively reaches into her pocket, where her L.G.D. badge is.
But the first thing she feels is something soft, the ribbon that Tessai gave her after removing it from Hannya. A gift from her friends, bearing their best wishes.
For some reason, she feels like she can smell the sea breeze of Siesta.
The haystacks of the Victorian plains.
The aroma of Lungmen's downtown restaurants.
...And the scent of Kajimachi's old wooden houses.
You're right.
Good, you've seen the light. Now hurry up and—
I mean, you're right that the L.G.D. would take care of things for me.
Maybe I've been too cautious... too concerned about bothering my friends.
What do you mean?
Hoshiguma does not answer. She takes the sword and sheath from her belt, and begins wrapping the ribbon around the handle.
You're going to use the sword? For what?
I have a promise to fulfill.
You're going to rescue them?
Don't be sil—

Missy would probably be angry enough to burst a few blood vessels if I just went back to Lungmen with my tail between my legs, assuming I had a tail. And I'd never be able to look Lin Yühsia in the eye again.
As for Ch'en... I still have to fight Chi Xiao with the reforged Hannya. I can't very well go back empty-handed.
Aren't you afraid that you'll regret it for the rest of your life, like twenty years ago?
Uncle Tessai said that Hannya was imbued with the secret art of the temples. But I didn't have the discipline to suppress the madness in my Oni blood.
Discipline, madness... don't you think all that's a little vague?
I don't know what would've happened twenty years ago if the secret art was there, and I don't care. I don't need it now.
As long as this ribbon touches my hand, Hoshiguma will never fall to bloodlust.
As for regrets, the only thing I regret is that I tried to shoulder everything myself, but I was obsessed my sacrifice meaning something.
Why do you think you have to do this? Is it because you're a cop, or because you're yakuza?
Cop? Yakuza?
None of that matters.
I promised Momoka, that's all.
Oh, and since I'm going to rescue them, it's more likely than not I'll run into Mifune at some point... and I'll go find him if I don't. There's something I have to ask him.
Hoshiguma sees a light begin to appear from within the policeman's sunken, world-weary eyes.
You'll fight him?
I'm not looking for a fight, but I'm not sure there's a way out of it.
Put a gash or two in him for me.
You don't even have to ask.
Is that enough, though?
Like I said, I'm a cop.
I'm going back to the station.
I'll do my best to fend off pressure from upstairs, as long as there are no corpses.
Words cannot express my gratitude.
I hope I live up to it.
Hoshiguma! Hoshiguma! Can you hear me?
Can you hear—
Quit yappin'!
Hoshi—
You know what, yap all you want, we'll give that aho a warm welcome!
What does Kinbee want with me?
Kinbee? Who's that?
I mean, Mifune.
How am I supposed to know? You're an idol, so maybe the oyabun has some use for you. Or maybe he just plans to do you in.
Should've buttered him up more with that pretty face of yours. I don't know what you're thinking.
Bakayaro, who the heck's texting me? Everyone knows I got assigned last-minute guard duty—
What? Some other unlucky sod's taking over the shift?
Ya—hoo!
He's gone.
What happened? Why did I feel like I was hearing voices... even talking to Hoshiguma?
Am I going crazy?
Behind—you—
Eek!
Pfft!
So you weren't acting in those shows. I didn't believe it when Kichisei told me.
You know Kichisei? Is she alright?
Locked up, like you, but probably safe for now.
Wh-Who are you?
You can call me Sarasa. I'm a fan of yours, and I guess you could say I'm Mifune's employee, but my true identity is an undercover cyber ninja!
Cyber... ninja? How's that different from a regular ninja?
Look, ninja are nothing more than hired killers. But the cyber kind? We're wanderers of the intercity net!
But I thought cyber ninja were pretty much the same, hired by corporations to attack each other, just specializing in intercity net operations?
Fine, I'm a cyber ronin ninja!
But I don't know where you came from, or why you're helping me—
That video that you saw at home, the one where Mifune and Mio plot to infect you with Oripathy? I was the one who leaked it to warn you! Do you trust me now?
You were the one who gave me that fright in the middle of the night?!
Well, it was the first time I worked on a Mitsukue—I mean, the Kinseki Channel signal system. It was a mistake. I didn't do it on purpose, I promise!
A-Alright... well, I guess I do owe you my life...
Honestly, I only applied to Kinseki Channel because I heard it was the biggest TV station in Mitsukue, and I needed a job.
But the longer I worked, the more things began to stink. But at least they had you as their poster girl... but they wouldn't even keep their hands off you!
Especially that Mifune. I would've just kept my head down and did the work I was paid for, if he wasn't so yabai.
But I'd rather jump into the sea than work another day for that piece of shit.
...Hehe.
I located Hoshiguma using Mifune's monitoring network, so I patched you in briefly.
You can do that? I remember how the technical director spent a long time fumbling with a pre-set connection the last time I was live on air...
That just means he sucks at his job.
Now, listen to me. I'll patch you in with Hoshiguma again, and have her wait outside.
We'll take Kichisei. Listen to me, and I'll make sure those stupid yakuza never even catch a glimpse of your shadow.
No, I can't go.
Are you crazy? Do you WANT Mifune to cut you up into burdenbeast feed?
Where can I go, if I leave?
The Southern Court's a big place. Plenty of places you can go.
Lay low the rest of my life, always looking over my shoulder? No way.
I didn't give myself the name Momoka Hanyuu, but if you tell me I have to give it up now...
How's that any different from being dead?
Are you serious? Aren't you afraid to die? Do you have a death wish or something?
I don't know. All I know is that it's the only path I have left.
Fine. You know what, that sounded really cool, like a line straight out of a movie. You're a star for a reason!
How do you plan on continuing to be a star, then? What if Kinbee won't let you?
Whoever stands in my way... they're gonna find out that even I can bite when backed into a corner.
Didn't Kinbee make up all those kaidan stories? I'll tell all Mitsukue that they've been lied to.
Interesting.
Can you connect me to every TV set in Mitsukue? I'm going to expose Kinbee to the entire city.
How long do you think it's gonna take, hacking all those TVs, one at a time?
But they're doing that accession ceremony and broadcasting it to the whole city. All we need to do is take a ride on it.
You still have that broken sword? It'll come in useful.
The first thing that Kinbee's people did when they captured us was take it from me...
But I'll say it, with or without the evidence.
Good.
I'll get that Kichisei girl, and Ao-Oni on the other side of the TV, and then we can talk about how we'll teach Mifune a lesson!
Where are you?
Where are... you...?
Did I doze off?
Wait, did the kami-sama...?!
I'm not in that much of a hurry.
It's you?!
Moriuchi sits straight with a start.
He wants to turn around, but dares not, afraid that he will learn that it is all a dream, and there is nothing there at all.
The kami seems satisfied with this reaction.
Kami-sama...
It... it's been a long time.
You're finally willing to show yourself to me.
I'm very grateful that you pulled my family out of poverty.
Did you spend all these years looking for me, just to say thank you?
I wanted to thank you, of course, but there was also something I wanted to ask.
Why... why did you leave us? Why was it that we lost our happy life, little by little, until it was all gone?
Did we offend you? Or was it fate that not even you could avert... and that was why you chose to avoid us?
What does any of that have to do with me?
I-I understand, of course it couldn't be...
What I mean is, did you spend all these years looking for me just to learn the reason?
Were you not looking for me because you wanted to seize happiness again?
I've said this many times, but I guess you were too young to remember, so I'll be patient and tell you once more.
I never give anyone anything.
Think of it this way: there's something I like, and I appear in front of people when I see it.
Something... do you mean, happiness?
Yes.
I didn't avoid you because I was angry at you, or ashamed to see you. I simply chose not to see you because happiness had left you.
But now, I have a reason to see you again.
Choose the right path, and you shall know happiness beyond your wildest dreams.
The kami's voice is exactly as he remembers, but for some reason it makes the hair on the back of his neck stand up. Is it because he has lost the innocence of childhood?
Is it because he has heard too many lies in his time?
Yes. He has heard too many lies, and he has learned to treat all beautiful promises as deception.
But this is different.
Please... tell me.
You stand in the eye of the storm.
The Oni, Hoshiguma; the snake, Kinbee. Add to that a poor goldfin, and a wolf who believes its own nose—you. All your fates are intertwined.
Oh, there was also an unfortunate old man, but he's dead now.
Tessai-san?!
With his death, you finally have the chance to seize the happiness that you have longed for.
His death... my happiness? What do you mean?
Why... why is Tessai dead? Who killed him?
Is it so hard to guess?
What you REALLY should ask yourself, though, is if it matters?
You stand where countless people could only dream of standing, Moriuchi. This is a business opportunity, a big one. Choose right, and everything will be yours.
You want me... to stand on Kinbee's side?
Kinbee is a liar. He wouldn't let me live.
That's why it's a big business opportunity.
Kinbee is a liar, certainly, but he needs you. More specifically, Kinsekikai needs you.
The separation of the Kinsekikai and the Kinseki Group has entered the final phase. Only a catastrophic event could stop it.
Everything seems to be going according to Kinbee's plan, but he's about to find that he isn't as powerful as he thought.
You've survived for so long between the overworld and underworld of Kajimachi and Nita Street. As a man, you're not that far off from Kinbee.
Moriuchi is stunned.
In his mind, he fantasied about his reunion with the Kami countless times, but never in another thousand years could he have imagined that It would so calmly tell him something so...
...Terrifying.
You want me to take Kinbee's place? Is that the happiness that you're talking about?
Yes.
What if I take his place, but find it beyond my abilities...?
But you'll be rich. Very rich.
Rich...?
You'll be rich, whether you have the ability or not. Maybe not as rich as Kinbee is... but the kind of wealth that you could hardly even imagine right now.
Is money not happiness?
Was it not money that you and your family found when you were young?
Is it not because of money that Mitsukue, and this country, enjoy endless happiness?
Where would happiness be if money left you?
...Is this the lesson you have for me?
It's not a lesson, just the simplest law of existence.
Kinbee knows it. The Diet knows it. Virtually everyone in Mitsukue knows it... you knew it too, when you were little.
Moriuchi feels a chill come over him, as though the setting sun is suffocating in its own golden rays.
Is this really the Kami that he encountered when he was young?
Or is this simply an absurd nightmare... no, something trying to deceive him and make him abandon his search for the kami?
But Its mannerisms, the way It talks, even the line 'What does any of that have to do with me?'... he has never forgotten them.
In Moriuchi's memory, even those cold words seemed to be a kind of encouragement. Now, though...
Moriuchi is gasping for air, but the cold nightmare seems inexorably suffocating.
He reaches into his pocket and takes out the key.
There are numerous scratches on the old-fashioned key, with a tiny maneki-neko hanging from the keychain.
It has kept him company for many years.
Money is happiness, you say?
Maybe. Maybe that's what I always thought, deep inside.
But did you ever stop to think... before all that happened, I only ever thought of you as a friend, the only friend of a lonely boy?
The friend brought me happiness, for which I was very grateful, but could never repay. You had no intention to accept everything that I did.
When you disappeared after I failed to come to see you for a while, I thought it must be because I had neglected you.
I thought I deserved it, because I had abandoned my only friend.
You know? I almost did the exact same thing when I was being coerced by Mifune.
The Oni?
Yes. My guts wrenched in shame when she came to rescue me, but I couldn't say anything.
But you just told me that I got it backwards all along. It wasn't you who brought me happiness. You simply came to me because happiness was there.
Yes.
I see. You're a kami... you've never been any more or less than a kami.
I'm sorry for my arrogance, to think that I could befriend a kami, never mind trying to catch its tail.
Thank you for coming to see me, and telling me that I have been walking down the wrong path my entire life.
How could an information broker have any true friends?
Moriuchi raises the keys and takes off the maneki-neko accessory.
He looks at the keychain, as though he is talking to the kami behind it.
But I wanted a friend. A true friend.
Can you understand that? Would it make you... angry?
If you're not angry... might I be so presumptuous as to... make a bet with you?
Moriuchi stretches out his hand, into the sky unobstructed by concrete.
If I do as you say...
I'll never have a true friend for the rest of my life...
I'll never have true happiness.
He bites his lip and opens up his trembling hand.
The accessory falls straight down, like any ordinary object, and is soon swallowed by the dark streets beneath the setting sun.
...Sayounara.
Where are you going?
To find Hoshiguma. If Tessai's dead, as you said, then I have to go and help Hoshiguma.
She's in the back alley of Nita Street. The one with that tiny shrine.
Huh... huh?
What are you huhing for? It's me?
Maneki-Neko-sama, It—
Moriuchi turns around to see nothing, as though nothing was there to begin with, except for the orange-yellow glow of dusk.
He turns his head to the front, and sees the Nosy Kami floating in the air in front.
That neko left because you rejected It. Does that surprise you?
Then... why did you help me?
It's not me who wanted to help you. The star of our shrine told me to help you if I have time, for all the chikuwa offerings you made.
That neko could've done it Itself! Hmph!
Thank you for the help, and pass on my thanks to that... er... 'star'?
Don't expect me to help you fight. I'm just pointing the way.
After all, both of us have a score to settle with that bastard Mifune.
Moriuchi-san?
Did you come to help me, or stop me?
Help.
Did you find your kami-sama, then?
It's doing fine. Much better than I am. But It has nothing to do with me, now that I've found It.
Where's Momoka? Isn't she with you?
I was about to go in and rescue her—
Moriuchi? Is that Moriuchi?
The TV?
Moriuchi-san! Momoka told me your oden's the best in Higashi! I want to try it, once this is all over and done with.
Who are you?
Call me Sarasa, but we don't have time for introductions right now!
Momoka, Kichisei, get closer to the mic! Time to make battle plans!
Mitsukue's in for a BIG surprise!
Gazing down from the top of the TV tower, one can see fireworks rise in the night sky before falling gently and disappearing from sight.
The fireworks show has not begun yet. This is simply the warmup for the warmup.
The Phidia gazes at his own reflection in the window, and that of the reforged Hannya behind him.
...Hannya.
I finally have you, after twenty years... too late. Too late.
What could you possibly do for me, other than acting as witness to this ceremony in Tessai's place?
As the Phidia gazes upon the reforged Hannya, he realizes that there are remnants of what seemed to be straps of some sort on the handle on the back side.
The back side is untouched?
A good shield. But nothing more.
Mio!
How long until the start of the ceremony?
Around an hour.
I'm going downstairs for some fresh air.
Hoshiguma may crash the ceremony, Mifune-san.
So much the better.
What do you mean?
If she does, it would mean one of two things: either she's been driven to madness, or she has somehow managed to stay calm after Tessai's death.
On my turf, in front of my camera, and on television, however, she'll be nothing more than a crazed murderer.
Nothing more than a footnote in the accession ceremony. A failure driven to madness. Even if I'm not a match for her, the ones upstairs will take care of her.
About Momoka...
Getting soft-hearted?
Well, we could certainly keep her around for a while longer, extract every last bit of value from her. We have plenty of ways to persuade her—
But we'll have to leave that Kichisei alive, too, so that Momoka is not driven to despair. You haven't dealt with her yet, have you?
No.
Good.
What about Tetsuya?
Mio?
...He escaped.
He what?
Mifune's voice turns sinister in an instant.
The truth.
...I let him go.
Soft-hearted, again?
Yes.
Is that all?
That's all.
I'm disappointed in you.
I'm prepared to accept my punishment.
It can wait. But this necessitates a change in your responsibilities.
You will face Hoshiguma, if she does come.
It's my duty.
Don't be in such a hurry to say yes.
You know what awaits if she shows up before me still living and breathing.
...Yes.
Two ninjas will keep a constant eye on you. Don't try anything funny.
Now, I need to get that fresh air. Make sure everyone is ready for any unexpected situations.
The fireworks show won't start for a while. Let's walk around.
No, darling, we should go straight to the site of the fireworks show on the other side of the river. They're saying on the intercity net that this area's not safe now.
Rumor has it that the oyabun of the Kinsekikai is dead, killed by the Ao-Oni, the one in the kaidan.
Oh—that media mogul? What's his name... Kin...
No, that's Mifune. It was Tessai who died.
Huh, you know, I actually thought Mifune was the one who died, since he's always showing up in public.
There's been rumors coming from Kajimachi, plus the Northern Court spy that they're talking about... anyway, we should go over to the other side of the river.
Kaidan. Hot topics. Manipulation of public opinion.
It has become second nature to him.
Who would have thought that all the kaidan of Mitsukue originated from Shimasu's attempt to blackmail him over the Diet candidate affair, only to end up slain by his sword?
Shimasu's death needed to be covered up. The fledgling TV station needed relevance. The diet member whom he was trying to butter up was waiting to see him prove his quality.
So he linked Hoshiguma with 'Sawara the Silentious' of Shin-Aki, and reported on the Haunted House for a week. It was a smashing success.
The intensive reporting dispelled any suspicions that viewers had, and left behind plenty of room for him to clean up and misdirect.
Relevance from the kaidan became money. Money moved diet members and shackled the police, made the Kinsekikai grovel before him, and rendered Tessai powerless to intervene.
Everything snowballed from there. The Haunted House became the ideal place to dispose of people who got in the way.
But the trick could not continue forever. Interest would fade, and revenue would decline, while Tessai's suspicions and the pressure of public opinion grew.
When the last gangster who knew how the Diet candidate died was dealt with, he began to expand the scope and channels of the 'kaidan'.
It could involve the kami or accidents, even a fabricated story that was sufficiently interesting and frightening.
He, too, wondered why the people of Mitsukue were so obsessed with kaidan.
They feared any potential danger in life, but they could not control their own curiosity. The prospect of danger seemed to represent a fatal attraction.
The TV station provided the Kinsekikai with a constant source of income, funding the crystalline component industry, making him someone whom Tessai looked down on, but could not do without him.
Meanwhile, to him, Tessai was already nothing more than a...
Emergency...

lines.st: Emergency, emergency.
This is not a drill. This is not a drill.

lines.x: What's going on? Earthquake?!

lines.st: Mitsukue is under attack by the Northern Court. Repeat, Mitsukue is under attack by the Northern Court.
For your own safety, please follow these instructions:

lines.x: What? A Northern Court invasion?!

lines.st: If you are inside, close all doors and windows and await further instructions.
If you are outside, go to the nearest shelter immediately.

lines.x: Sh-Shelter? Do we have those in Mitsukue?
There used to be announcements, but now... we'll have to look for the signs...
On Nita Street and beyond, all of Mitsukue is suddenly filled with red lights, with the exception of the oblivious fireworks.
A fear that was once forgotten has been reawakened.
Conflict between North and South has only increased since the Bloodpeak Campaign, but rapid economic growth caused the attention of the people to drift away from the gunfire on the border.
Mizukue and Kusarigawa continued to treat each other as potential adversaries, even after the '90s, but the average folk have long since forgotten the possibility.
This fear, which has lain forgotten for many years but never truly left, seized the hearts of Mitsukue's people more powerfully than any kaidan.
Mifune-san!
What the blazes is going on?!
Someone hacked into Kinseki Channel's broadcasting network! They tampered with the backup emergency footage provided by the government and are broadcasting it all over the city!
We're not sure how, but they even managed to take over the networks of some of the other stations. Even our partners are affected.
They're spamming our phone lines, thinking that we approved it!
Moshimoshi? What's going on—
It's the Diet?!
How's the infiltration going?
Couldn't be better. Total chaos right now. Had no difficulty breaching.
Don't go too far, Sarasa. There might be unintended consequences to spreading fake news.
Don't worry, I avoided crowded places like the fireworks show, and places like hospitals, schools, and old folks' homes, where people tend to have weak hearts.
I also set a retraction video to play after the warning's looped a few times, telling people that it was a mistake.
I'll go to the ceremony now with Momoka and Kichisei.
Time for you to create more chaos, Hoshiguma.
On it.
Hoshiguma walks in the opposite direction of the panicking crowd, towards the TV station.
As Sarasa said, the broadcasts are now changing to play the retraction video, and the crowd gradually begins to calm down.
As Hoshiguma walks, she wonders if she might see the reaction she hoped for from the crowd.
It need not be an awakening or an awareness of vigilance, nothing so heavy.
She just wants to see them shiver and remember what fear truly is, not just a little punctuation to spice up a happy life.
But all she hears is people excitedly repeating a single word...
'Kaidan.'
Here we are.
So little security? Looks like the station really has its hands full.
The one-horned Oni walks inside without hesitation, passing by one, two, and three station staff without being noticed.
Until the fourth...
The Ao-Oni?!
It's the Ao-Oni! Call the Kinsekikai!
I suggest you make way while I'm still asking politely.
No?
They have her surrounded. They outnumber her many to one. Yet, for some reason, there is a thought in all their minds that they cannot shake...
She outmatches them—one against many.
This is for your own good. Maybe some of you have heard about what the 'Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai' did twenty years ago, right here in Mitsukue.
Even if we're no longer clan-family, even if your oyabun... killed Uncle Tessai, I don't want to fight you, if I don't have to.
Make way. I want to see Kinbee. Don't make me do this.
Y-You won't get away!
The Kinsekikai never issued a notice of 'expulsion' or 'no-contact' to you! You're still part of the Kinsekikai, and to attack us is to attack your own family!
Do you really believe that?
No one answers.
Hoshiguma continues walking, almost nonchalantly.
There is no sound of footsteps behind her, only a slight whoosh of something flying through the air.
She does not even cast a glance at it. Be it knife or flash drive, shuriken or just a handful of beans, none of that matters anymore.
When you're throwing a knife, you really should throw it like you mean it. You might as well have been throwing roasted soybeans.
Oni wa soto, fuku wa uchi, that's the old saying? Oni out, fortune in?
Surrender, and we can keep it at that. There's no bad blood between us.
No bad blood? Maybe you're right. But back in my day, we had a different slogan when we threw beans in Kajimachi.
We said, 'Fortune out...
'Oni in.'
She draws her sword.
The yakuza who have heard of the Ao-Oni instinctively take a step backwards. Only one of the younger members steps forward, seemingly oblivious to what he is risking for a chance at glory.
Alright then.
No one saw what the one-horned Oni did. All they saw was a blue flash, and the young yakuza fell to the ground, a bloody gash on his thigh.
Th-There's only one of her! Get her!
We're the Kinsekikai! We're the strongest! Get her! We must not disappoint the Kinsekikai or Chairman Mifune!
That's more like it.
They rush toward the striding Oni like the tide.
The Oni takes step after step forward, until the distance between her and the figure ahead closes to a hair's breadth—
Once again, the blue light, but this time, everyone sees the true nature of the Oni, no longer attempting to suppress her fury.
Hoshiguma's blade moves through the air.
Before long, there is no longer anyone standing in her way. The injuries are not fatal, but no one has the strength, or the stomach, to oppose her anymore. Only the one-horned Oni still stands tall.
Sad.
The TV station's in total chaos...
Wow...
Grandma told me stories about Northern Court special forces infiltrating Mitsukue in her youth.
I didn't think much of it at the time. Thinking about it now, though, what we're seeing is just a fraction of what could really happen...
What do you think about my surprise for Kinbee?
I think it might be a little too big...
Well, we should focus on our own tasks. Moriuchi-san has already infiltrated the TV station. We need to head to the accession ceremony.
There shouldn't be many people there at this time, but you'll need to help out if there's any resistance.
Gotcha.
All that's left to do is act! I'll lead the way. Climb up this vent!
Follow me!
I-I'm coming!
It... it's so high...
Don't worry, I've got a light. Hold my hand, and I'll lead you on.
Momoka tries not to look down, but she cannot keep herself from shaking.
They are moving through massive formations of steel rebar. The darkness of night hangs above, while it is bright as day underneath their feet.
TV station staff, yakuza, and ninja form a picture of chaos beneath them.
How are you doing, Kichisei?
A-Alright.
Good.
You don't come up to this kind of place often?
Who does? Are you a deserting ninja or something?
No, I got used to this kind of place while hiding from ninjas.
Hiding?
That's not important—we're here!
Hmph, we've got people down below...
Momoka, jump over this gap and follow those pipes down to the stage. You should be able to stay out of their view.
That far?!
I, I'll try...
Momoka clenches her fists, her eyes fixed forward, and kicks the ground hard—
The steel structure suddenly starts moving, and the platform beneath Momoka's feet begins to vibrate irregularly.
Why did it start moving all of a sudden?!
I, I think I'm falling!
Watch your head!

What's going on...?
Whoa!
She fell?!
We're going down too! Sarasa!
Ya—hoo!
Are you alright, Momoka?
I'm alright! Hanging in there.
Get on the stage! I'll get the console! Start once the lights come on!
I can't wait for Momoka's show—
I'm afraid I can't let you do that.
Mio-san...?
...Momoka.
You've come to stop me, haven't you?
Yes.
Why?
For the Kinsekikai.
I know what you're going to say. That will ruin the Kinsekikai.
Mio-san, Hoshiguma has shown up at the door of the TV station. You should go now, according to Mifune-san's instructions—
Hoshiguma showed up for her.
But Mifune-san—
The other one watching me, I know you're there. Go and tell Mifune-san that Hoshiguma will definitely come, and I'll be waiting for her here.
Now, Momoka, Kichisei-san, and that girl hiding behind the console, whose name I do not know.
I suggest you stay put until Hoshiguma shows up.
Your grievances against Mifune-san and Kinsekikai are certainly legitimate. But this place is also home to me, and to many others.
I can't let you destroy it.
She's bluffing! Momoka—
The black ethereal hand suddenly appears in front of Kichisei's neck. She instinctively takes a step back and pulls the trigger.
The ethereal hand shreds the bead-shaped grenade.
The lights go out, and the stage darkens, illuminated only by a faint light in the direction of the console.
Mio walks towards the light, sword in one hand, tantou in the other.
That's right, I'm here... come on now...
One of the spotlights suddenly turns on, shining straight into Mio's eyes. Then another, and another, and another—
Then they all turn off at the same time.
Mio's hesitation lasts only an instant. She takes a moment to collect herself, then charges at the console—
Bang!
A falling spotlight narrowly misses Mio's head. By the time she turns her gaze back to the control console, there is no longer anyone there.
While Mio is occupied with the console, Kichisei, Momoka and Sarasa begin running towards the side stage exit.
She locked the door! Let's try another—
Click, click, click.
They hear the sounds of locks being engaged...
The lights above their head turn back on.
Mio slowly walks towards the gathered trio.
The lights are no longer under your control. Resistance is futile.
You think so?
Sarasa closes her eyes and raises her lamp-shaped staff.
Mio's pace quickens.
Suddenly, the tip of the staff emits a blinding light, and the spotlights above the stage turn off again.
Not just the spotlights. All the light that remains on the stage is trapped in the liquid bouncing at the tip of the staff—
Lights out, ice queen!
—and even the last glimmer of light is utterly consumed.
Sarasa?!
Let... me sleep... I'm... so tired...
(Whisper) Wh-What do we do? I can hear Mio-san's footsteps! She's coming!
(Whisper) Let's try the other side of the stage. Maybe the door there's still unlocked...
(Whisper) No, it's too far. She'll find us!
(Whisper) This is where we fell, isn't it? Can we go back the way we came?
(Whisper) The vents—
Kichisei puts Sarasa on her back and was about to move, when they hear Mio's voice coming from center stage.
Don't bother. I can hear you.
What do we do?!
It's all or nothing. I'll deal with her—
Suddenly, she hears the sound of knocking behind her.
Is it locked? I guess we'll have to do this the hard way.
Get out of the way!
A massive crash, and the locked door swings open, bringing in light from beyond.
The next instant, as though answering the call of the light coming in from the door, bright orbs of light float up from Sarasa's staff and go back to where they came from—
...Sarasa?
I thought I would get to sleep for a little longer...
There'll be no light here as long as I'm still asleep...
Sarasa yawns, and the spotlights above flicker.
Mio does not move from her position, so Sarasa begins walking towards the console.
Momoka follows hesitantly, but Mio remains in place.
Aren't you going to stop them?
No. Your blade is drawn.
My duty is not to deal with them.
I doubt your minions have what it takes to beat Kichisei and Sarasa.
In that case, there's nothing I can do.
What about Mifune?
I don't care.
You...
Our battle has nothing to do with him.
The last TV station's back online, Detective Koretou!
I hear you.
The guys assigned to the fireworks show want to know what to do. The citizens are still uneasy after that scare.
Any risk of a stampede or other serious accident?
Minimal. The situation's very much within expected parameters.
Then the show will go on. Mifune likes excitement. We'll give him excitement.
We're getting calls from citizens asking what happened. What do you think...?
What do you think I think? Tell them we're investigating.
Kinseki Station is, of course, responsible for the erroneous announcement of a Northern Court invasion, and we're taking this very seriously—
Detective Koretou, you have a visitor!
Who? Kouhei Mifune?
Detective Koretou.
If it wasn't Mifune, I thought it would have been his Diet friend paying a personal visit.
Making insinuations will do no favors for your career. Besides, you overestimate your own importance.
Should I take that as an admission?
I've come to warn you.
The police must bear responsibility for the chaos that occurred on Nita Street tonight. We do not wish to see the chaos spread any further.
Bear responsibility, eh? Oh dear, I think I just peed my pants.
Watch your attitude. You still have a chance to settle this matter peacefully.
Do I? Pray enlighten me.
Cancel all events scheduled for tonight. Focus your forces on restoring order at Kinseki Station, and begin a search for those responsible.
Such as? The number one suspect is, I'm sure, Kouhei Mifune, considering that it was his station that broadcast that—
Your men are not allowed to touch him—
Have a nice day.
If you refuse to obey orders, you may go once you have signed this resignation.
Feel free to discuss any concerns with your supervisor.
Wow, I still have a chance to turn back?
It's too late for that.
Like you said, it's too late for that—
The detective reaches out with his foot...
You—
...He trips the visitor, takes the piece of paper with the name of the detective's supervisor on it, and tears it to pieces.
Oh my, I didn't expect you to fall like that. I thought our diet friend would have a tougher secretary.
How... how dare you?!
Go tell your boss that the police are here to protect his bloody life, not his bloody money.
If he really thinks someone's after his life, he can come personally to the station and file a report, and we'll take it into consideration.
Now, get lost.
White sword, black gloves, white hand, black sword, white tantou, black ethereal hand—

It is a dizzying exchange of black and white. Contrary to the impression she gives, the Liberi's swordplay is overwhelmingly aggressive, with only attacks, feints and more attacks, with not the slightest attention paid to defense.
The moves of the one-horned Oni, on the other hand, are almost staggeringly basic. Dodge, parry, backstep, sidestep.
Twelve.
You just blocked twelve attacks in quick succession.
Why don't you fight back? Are you still afraid of your sword?
If I was, it wouldn't have left its sheath to begin with.
You, on the other hand, look all polite and cultured, but your moves are pure aggression and ferocity. That's not Kinbee's style.
My sword has nothing to do with him.
Then why do you give your life for him?
Since when have I given my life for him?
Stop fooling yourself.
Hoshiguma takes a deliberate look around.
Kichisei is sending explosions everywhere with her grenades, while the stage equipment has turned into a giant monster under Sarasa's control.
With Momoka doing an impressive job of not getting caught, the trio is more than holding their own.
There is only one last thing to do in their battle plan...
The only thing I'm trying to protect is the Kinsekikai.
And Kinbee has to lead it?
The Kinsekikai needs to be a place where the dregs of society can survive, those who have nowhere else to go.
And Tessai couldn't give you that?
No.
You can keep parrying—
But you'll eventually show an opening.
Finally, you're attacking.
All I got was the edge of your clothes.
There is blue fire burning around you. Is that your Arts?
Blue fire?
You're right. I didn't notice.
Back to the question. What is it that Tessai couldn't give you, but Kinbee can?
Tessai could not even protect himself—
Hoshiguma thrusts. The attack is simple, but the flames upon the sword force Mio to leap backwards.
That's not what I wanted to hear.
Your flames are burning even fiercer.
Tell me. Why couldn't Tessai give you what you wanted?
Because Tessai couldn't protect himself. Mitsukue didn't need him, nor did it need a Mio working under Tessai.
You?
A silent, ragged orphan who only fights tooth and nail when someone steps on her is a subject of scorn.
An equally silent, well-dressed assistant who only draws her sword when the company's interests are compromised is the subject of admiration.
I thought you came from some noble martial family fallen on hard times.
Hah.
Tessai wanted to give dregs of society like us a home, and so he's dead.
Mifune knows we don't deserve a home, and so he lives on.
Is that how Mitsukue judges the Kinsekikai?
Always has.
Hoshiguma is almost completely engulfed in the azure flame.
The Liberi wonders if the flame will consume the Oni before it consumes her.
You are angry.
Were you angry like this too, twenty years ago?
Does that matter?
Mifune-san wants you to lose your reason, as you did twenty years ago. He believes it will turn you into a crazed beast, one that everyone will be eager to put down.
You know this is what he wants, and so you suppress yourself, which is why you didn't draw your sword the last time.
...Hmph.
Are you inviting me to give in to anger? Complete the mission your master assigned?
It's not what I want, but I would have no issue with it. It would be a shame, that's all.
Why?
A perfect opponent, who could never stand before me in perfect condition, forced to either suppress herself, or lose control entirely.
But that's all.
Take this.
Mio once again prepares to attack.
Three hands, three swords, a posture seemingly without form or order, but ready to pounce on any weakness—
Catch!
Who's there?!
This is crazy heavy!
You know how hard it was to get it out of Kinbee's room, even with all that chaos at the TV station?
...Catch!
Moriuchi swings his entire body to throw Hannya in the direction of the standoff.

Mio's ethereal hand is the first to catch Hannya's handle, but Hoshiguma leaps and spins one hundred and eighty degrees like she is dancing, forcing the ethereal hand to loosen its grip, allowing her to take Hannya's handle.
Suppress, or lose control?
It won't happen. With or without Hannya.
Hoshiguma tightens her grip around the ribbon on her sword.
Hannya's an old friend, after all. Maybe you had a chance just now... but not any longer.
Rage.
The rage that seemed compressed around Hoshiguma now spreads outwards, burning on her arms, her shield, and her sword.
Hoshiguma takes a step forward, and the flames of rage almost catch the hem of Mio's clothing.
Good.
It's good enough for me to know that you won't lose control.
Hoshiguma faces off against black and white, hands human and not, swords long and short.
The Liberi shows no fear. The edges of her mouth angle ever so slightly upwards.
Any last words?
Be kind to the Kinsekikai.
Be kind to Momoka.
My conscience is clear on all other matters... except her.
I'll remember.
What about Kinbee? Any last words about him you want to say?
Him? Is there any need to talk about him, here and now?
Hah. Good point.
Hoshiguma shifts her posture to one that makes it easy to unleash her power. The blue flames continue to burn intermittently around her.
The face of Hannya glares with wide-open eyes on the face of the shield. The edge of the sword seems ready to seep blue blood—
Hah!
Is this... Hannya?
Yes, this is Hannya.
Your swords are worthy of you. They took the full brunt of Hannya and survived.
You can pick them up, then we'll continue.
There's no need.
You've made your strength clear. There's no point in trying to fight you anymore.
I must prepare.
For what?
For the aftermath.
For a Kinsekikai without Mifune-san.
How's things on Momoka's side, Moriuchi? Are they ready?
Just waiting for you to finish fighting. Sarasa?
Aye aye, sir! Momoka?
Momoka stands in the middle of the stage with the spotlight on her.
Kichisei has done her best to clear a space, though there are still a few yakuza lying on the ground near her.
I... I'm ready to begin!
Alright then...
Kinseki Station's biggest program of the night begins now!
Yes, it's me! I just came back from our Diet friend's place!
Where is Hoshiguma?
The venue?!
What about Mio? Has she dealt with them?
No?! She left?
That traitor!
What about the network? Did you record what's going on inside? What about Hoshiguma?
Dammit! Get the editors on it NOW and make the footage of her fight with Mio look like she's completely lost control—
What do you mean there's no time?! Hacking? Hijacked network?! Can't deal with it?
What good are you?! Cut the signal if you can't deal with it! The power supply too! The only thing left to air tonight is the apology! Who cares if the signal goes out?
We can't have someone babbling on MY channel—
Hello? Hello?!
Chikusho! Lost the signal?
You idiot! You demand I come and give a personal explanation, that just gives my enemies a chance to strike!
Everything's gone to hell while I was humoring that bastard... even Hannya is gone...
(Takes a deep breath)
Calm down.
Clean up the mess and start over. It'll be alright. It's not the first time.
First, go to the venue and stop those insects. The diet has given the nod on Hoshiguma. All that's left is to clean up public opinion...
Came to cause trouble with your dear Hoshiguma-aneki, have you?
Hoshiguma?
Oh my goodness. Should I compliment you for your bravery, or your adorable stupidity?
Look at how beat-up you are. Seems like my men were still playing with you, not knowing that you no longer had any use.
Who did you grab this real sword from? Tell me, and I'll return it to him before he gets angry.
Kinbee... you...
Me?
I don't have time to play with you, Tetsuya.
Your father may have taught you the basics of swordsmanship when you were little, but you have a long, long way to go before you're ready to use it in a real fight.
A father and son who got turned against each other by a mask. How much do you think you actually understood about the swordplay that he taught you?
Turned against?!
Yes... turned against each other...
I've said all I needed to say. Now get lost before I change my mind and reunite you with Tessai.
You're the one who should be reunited with my father!

lines.st: Deep breath.
I know. Countless people are watching me. I must tell them the truth.
But... does it mean anything?
They love Momoka Hanyuu. They love to watch her panic at all the kaidan that seem to orbit around her.
They love to watch me get scared, not speak the truth, and certainly not the real me...
Yes, I'm afraid of even that.
I'm a coward.
Being afraid is the biggest common denominator between Haruka Shino and Momoka Hanyuu.

lines.x: Hello everyone, good... good evening.
I... I'm Momoka Hanyuu... the girl who's so afraid of kaidan, but who just couldn't seem to get away from them.
I'm sorry for disturbing you all, after that big accident that just occurred on air...
Momo-cchi?!
Didn't she go missing? How did she suddenly pop up like that?
Was that emergency broadcast really a kaidan?
Is she caught in the middle of a kaidan... and looking for help from us?!
As you can see, I'm standing here before you.
Strange things seem to happen to me all the time. That's how kaidan are: they appear without warning, and disappear the same way.
But what if I told you that all the kaidan were fake, from the very beginning?
Momoka? Kinseki Channel's really throwing money into her, pulling that disappearing act. What's the kaidan this time?
She said they're fake.
What? Is she out of her mind? Does she think we're all stupid? Aren't kaidan all around us all the time?
That's what she said. Don't ask me, watch her yourself.
What if I told you that, with the exception of a handful of kaidan associated with the kami, all the shows that I've been on, all the kaidan that I experienced... all of them were fabricated?
I'm not talking about hypotheticals. I'm talking about true lies.
Kinseki Station and the Kinseki Talent Agency deceived you, and I unknowingly became their accomplice.
I'm sorry.
I won't make any excuses for deceiving you. I'll accept any criticism.
I told you, no TV tonight!
My classmate told me they're airing something really amazing! Look! The kaidan lady spills the beans on fake kaidan!
I've been a part... of countless lies.
For example, the original kaidan, the family home of the Ao-Oni of the Kinsekikai in Kajimachi that killed its residents.
It was no paranormal activity, just actual murder.
I now accuse Kouhei Mifune, president of Kinseki Channel and the Kinseki Talent Agency, and the de facto leader of the Kinsekikai yakuza syndicate, of being the mastermind behind all this.
Without any evidence, he suspected me of being in league with his political opponent, Tessai, and plotted to infect me with Oripathy, when I knew nothing of any of it.
The 'Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai' was exiled from Mitsukue after taking the fall for his crimes, many years ago. She was framed for my kidnapping, when in fact, she had saved my life.
When I accidentally stumbled upon the truth behind the kaidan, he wanted to silence me, spreading rumors that I was retiring...
Detective Koretou! Upstairs is absolutely fuming!
This is the eighth time they're ordering us to cut the signal...
What did we do with the last seven orders?
...Nothing.
You've got it.
I know you've been unhappy about me saying that things will get better. I stopped believing it myself at some point.
Whatever happens tonight is on me. Let there be one night when the police don't have to grovel to anyone. It's my parting gift to you.
...This is what I needed to tell you tonight.
Next, there's something else I want to tell you... about myself.
I know a lot of people think I'm overacting in the shows... you think I'm being dramatic on purpose.
What I want to tell you is that fear has been a constant in Momoka Hanyuu's entire life.
Partly, it's because I was told too many horror stories when I was little, but...
The more important reason is that I grew up in Gokawa, the city infamous for its polluted water.
There, fear is not about serial killers roaming the streets at night, unicycles that bring misfortune, or elevators to hell...
It is about watching your loved ones becoming bedridden all of a sudden, falling while walking on flat ground, and losing the feeling in their hands.
It all happens so naturally, so gradually... and it's too late by the time you realize it.
I was one of the lucky ones. I came to Mitsukue, to be bathed in people's attention, be it adoring, judging, or scornful.
My fear was no longer about pain, illness and death; it was about viewer ratings, losing fans, pressure from the agency, and fabricated kaidan.
It seems such a luxury to fear those things, now that my life is once again under threat.
Isn't it a sort of happiness, to be afraid of kaidan?
We don't need to face true danger. We lie in our beds, screaming at stories we don't know if they're true, and satisfy ourselves with the sensation of our nerves tightening and relaxing again.
But... I'm afraid I can never enjoy it the same way again.
This luxury was lost to me forever when I learned that the kaidan that used to scare me were lies, manifestations of greed, murders, and cover-ups.
From this day forth, kaidan shall no longer be part of Momoka Hanyuu's life.
I hereby announce that I am leaving the Kinseki Talent Agency, and disassociating myself from all Kinsekikai affiliates!
Tetsuya is lying on the ground next to Mifune, wounded but alive.
It is not that the Phidia intended to spare him.

Indeed, Mifune wanted to put an end to this quickly many times, only to be forced back by the suicidal attacks of the young Oni, who seemed to be intentionally aiming himself at the tip of Mifune's sword, just to find a chance to take Mifune down with him.
Momoka's speech coming through the screen beside him only served as a further distraction, and his trembling hands repeatedly missed their mark.

Even as Mifune changed his goal from killing to incapacitation, and then to simply shaking off this maniac in order to deal with the fallout, Tetsuya kept getting up from the ground, in spite of all his injuries.
Meanwhile, Momoka's speech is drawing to a close.
I will continue to walk the performer's path, as long as I live.
I want to bring hope to others, and tell those plagued by fear that there is someone who was once just like you, someone who knows how you feel.
She will sing your story, act your story. She will do all that she can, so that your voices will not fade into darkness.
Momoka wipes away a drop of sweat from her brow.
Wow, I really said a lot, didn't I?
You know, I'm starting to get cold feet now, after having said all that. I'm really timid, after all.
Maybe the kaidan aren't the only lies in our lives. Maybe there are countless more lies like theirs, and the prosperity that we enjoy...
...is built upon these lies.
What is that bitch babbling about?!
She doesn't know that everything is built upon faith. She doesn't know that if panic spreads—
But... this is our life. And it will continue, until the end arrives... if it arrives.
Even if it is built upon lies... upon bubbles that might shatter tomorrow, it's still our life...
A life that I have to learn to love.
Besides, even if a life like this is destined to disappear someday... it's beautiful, isn't it?
An instant of beauty will be the warmth that stays with us through the cold night, even after everything else falls apart... as long as we keep it in our hearts.
That's all I want to say.
This has been Momoka Hanyuu, and I apologize for taking up your valuable time.
Perhaps... no, we WILL meet again.
There is no longer any possibility of turning the tables.
The Phidia stares blankly at the figure on the screen.
Oh, you got up again.
Shall we continue?
The bitch is done babbling. You can try to take revenge for your pop now, again.
The young Oni sets his unsteady feet. The Phidia has collected himself, and identifies an opening that will let him put his sword right through the ribs of the Oni when he makes his move.
But the Oni does not move.
Are you scared?
No.
But even a fool like me can tell that you're finished.
Tetsuya turns around. Mifune's face is ghastly pale, but he does not make a move.
I'm showing my back to you. Aren't you going to kill me?
Not in the mood. There are plenty of other people I want to kill first.
I'll come find you when they're all dead.
Oh, and one more thing...
Mifune stares at Tetsuya's back. Suddenly, he blurts out a sentence that even he himself is unsure why he says it.
The words that I said to your father... They were actually for you.
Tetsuya cannot tell if these words are meant to provoke, or to accomplish something else. He turns and casts one glare at Mifune with bloodshot eyes, before leaving with unsteady steps.
Phew...
Everything feels so surreal, like a dream.
After finishing her speech, she fainted onstage out of excitement.
Everything that happened next was like a blur... in any case, Hoshiguma went upstairs, while Moriuchi, Kichisei, and Sarasa took her downstairs.
She seems to remember fighting and explosions, but it all felt so far away—
Where are we? Kichisei? Moriuchi-san? Sarasa?
Why is Sarasa on the ground... she's asleep?!
She fell asleep in the TV station too... does she get tired easy?
No time for this—it's my turn to carry her now!
Where are you, Momoka? Where's Sarasa?
Did you get lost in the alleys?
I'm—
Mmph!
Gotcha!
I... I got her! On my own! I-I might get promoted for this...
Good thing I had duct tape on me. She's a singer, who knows how loud she can get when she hollers?
I dreamed about 'ninjutsu items' before I became a ninja, but it's only the veterans who get to use the good stuff, while all I have is trash...
Gotta report this upstairs—
Gah, I forgot I discharged my terminal so I could enjoy an afternoon of peace and quiet in one of the coffee shops in the alleys.
Then this girl just falls into my hands like that! It's definitely my lucky day.
Where's my Originium Circuit Hyourougan?
The ninja takes out the Originium Circuit Hyourougan, a spherical Originium appliance with a long wire attached to it, which he connects to the depleted terminal.
It's charging so slowly.
Hey, how about I pull that tape off your mouth, and you sing a song or two for me?
Mmph, mm, mmmh!
Well, it's not like I would do it, even if you nodded.
Did you know? We were looking for you everywhere after you ran away on that motorbike that night. I was the one who searched your house.
Mmh!
Boy, was that a crappy place. Big idol like you, I thought you'd be living in a mansion or something, but you didn't have anything worth shit.
What's with the collection of garbage by your window, anyway? Some kind of weird obsession?
Mmph—argh! *cough* *cough*!
Say that again?!
The tape came loose? I thought I put it on right.
Hmph, who cares, just a frail little idol.
I tell you, not only did I have to check all that weird garbage you had by the window one by one, I also trimmed that dying plant for you.
You cut Hasuke?!
'Hasuke'? Oh, that tree sprout was barely even a weed.

Yeah, I did it. What about it? How did you even take care of it? I have no idea, 'cause its leaves were all yellow and brittle. No way it was gonna live, so I put it out of its misery. It was a kindness.
I've grown flowers before, and it takes patience, and endless love and care, you understand? Someone like you—
Hey, hey, hey, how did you get up? What kind of crappy duct tape was that?
I see, you must know some sort of Arts... you're Ægir, so I guess it's about water? That's it, of course, no wonder the tape came loose.
But didn't the vets say that only the Infected could use Arts without a unit? That's weird. You couldn't have broken the tape with brute force, could you?
Nah, of course not. I doubt you've ever lifted anything heavier than an apple! Must be Arts, definitely.
So what? What are you gonna do, spray water in my face?
The novice ninja taunts Momoka, but he keeps his gaze fixed on her eyes.
A cornered beast will fight back, and the same is true for the girl in front of him. What kind of Arts will she use? Mist? A high-pressure jet? Acid spray, even?
He must not let his guard down just because he is facing an idol with no combat experience.
It is unlikely that her Arts would be particularly powerful, and there would be little movement from her microphone-shaped Arts unit when she casts. Better to stay focused on her eyes.
Her eyes will betray all that she is thinking.
For example... murderous intent.
Foolish girl, not knowing her place.
But that is all. Observe her eyes, and analyze her intent, be it the timing of casting, direction, or...
Why is there no intent other than murder?
Before he can react, the ninja feels a dull 'thud' on his head.
Wha-you hit me on the head with your mic? What kind of Arts is...?
MURDERER!
BEAST!
HEARTLESS DEVIL!
THIS IS FOR HASUKE!
W-Wait, calm down—
NO I WILL NOT!
IT WASN'T DEAD YET! HOW COULD YOU—
HOW COULD YOU CUT MY HASUKE?!
Momoka babbles her incoherent vengeful fury, a far cry from the image that she presented on stage just moments ago.
Her microphone finds the ninja, again and again, striking with a fury that even hell does not know...
Stop, stop, no—
I WILL KILL YOU, YOU BASTARD! I WILL BEAT THIS HEARTLESS MONSTER TO DEATH!!!
Momoka?! What are you doing?
I'M KILLING THE BASTARD WHO KILLED MY HASUKE! I WILL RIP HIM FROM LIMB TO LIMB!!!
So, you're, er... you're fine?
I'M FINE! I'M VERY FINE! I'M GONNA BEAT THIS ASSHOLE INTO A PULP!!!
M-Momoka, calm down, he's not worth it, we gotta get out of here...
The rain of blows finally comes to an end, as footsteps fade into the distance.
That other voice must have belonged to Kichisei, the one who was with Momoka. Yes, it was Kichisei who took Momoka away, it must be her...
Thank you, Kichisei... you're my lucky star—
The novice ninja gives Kichisei his most sincere gratitude before passing out. Not that he is in any condition to verbalize it.
I've been waiting for you. No one can stop you, even after all these years.
Quit posturing. Your men have already scattered. I strolled right on in here.
What business does the 'Ao-Oni of Kinsekikai' have with a man who's already finished?
If you came to take revenge for yourself and Tessai, all you need to do is draw your sword.
Hoshiguma shakes her head.
Your crimes have been exposed on your own TV station. Your Diet member upstairs won't help you now, no matter how much bribe money he got from you.
How he may try to cover up his ill-gotten gains is none of my business.
It's enough for me that justice will be served for the people you actually killed.
You almost sound like a cop.
Yeah, I've been working for the Lungmen police force.
...Are you serious?
Yes, I am.
I want to laugh, but I'm not quite in the mood tonight. And I thought the L.G.D. was just a gimmick.
I only came to do what I should have done when I took that step twenty years ago. I came to cut my last ties to Higashi.
And how do you intend to do that? You can't cut yourself off from your past. Karma and fate will always follow you.
You could put them in chains, lock them in boxes, but they will escape when you're least prepared, and tear everything into pieces.
The only thing you can cut is my throat, and it would be nothing more than a formality if you did so. Nothing more than venting your anger.
Yeah, a formality.
There's nothing I want from you or Mitsukue. All I want is a formality.
A letter of excommunication. Tell all of Mitsukue that Hoshiguma no longer has anything to do with Mitsukue, the Kinsekikai, or the Ao-Oni kaidan.
The tall Phidia blinks, then begins laughing wryly.
I see Lungmen has indeed turned you into a cop, through and through.
But I refuse. We have a better way of disassociating someone.
Cross swords with me. Rebel against the oyabun of the Kinsekikai, and there will be no room left for the Kinsekikai to treat you as one of us, no matter which one of us falls.
Do we really have to do this?
This is the true meaning of finishing unfinished business from twenty years ago.
It's just...
Mifune casts a glance around his office.
I rather liked this place, and it would be a shame if a mess was made of it. Let's go somewhere else.
Where?
Kajimachi.
You've razed it to the ground.
Our memories remain, don't they?
You're one twisted bastard.
Let's go. Maybe we'll even catch the end of the fireworks show.
No, Madame Oni! I can't let you go any further!
I have to save him.
Madame Oni!
There is no time. I'll only say once more. Get out of my way.
Are you really Madame Oni? Is there any humanity left in you?
Why... why wouldn't I be Madame Oni?
Shuuji is dead! You killed him!
Shuu...ji...?
You're not Madame Oni anymore... you're just an Oni... a vicious Oni, even...
You would kill the brother you drank at the same table with, and you wouldn't even hesitate, as long as we're in your way...
Will you kill me too, vicious Oni?
Out of the way... Koutarou.
I can't turn back. I must stop Kinbee, no matter what!
Vicious Oni!!!
She instinctively raises her sword, pointing it in front of her.
The man charges towards her, but the sword goes through his chest, as though he has intentionally impaled himself upon it.
Did he do it on purpose? Or did she parry his blade, then...
Vicious Oni...
Go... to hell...
Vicious Oni?
Am I... a vicious Oni?
Oni?
Blood fills her eyes, blinding her.
She will cut down anyone who charges at her.
She will strike back at any weapon that strikes at her.
More cries, the sound of weapons cutting through the night, discombobulated sounds that blend into a single lump in her chest, beating with her heart, pulsing—
Until it fully synchronizes with her heartbeat, drowning out all other sounds.
In the bloody haze, the vicious Oni keeps walking and killing, leaving behind a trail of blood and bodies, until she reaches the run-down shack and kicks the door open.
There is no sound there.
The vicious Oni stands at the door, cocking her head, as though she is listening for some kind of sound coming from hell that humans cannot hear.
Until she hears a trembling voice.
...dead.
Who's dead?
The Diet candidate... is dead.
The Diet candidate is dead?
The cries fade. The sound of weapons cutting through air fades. The sound of heartbeat fades. All the sounds grow distant and fade.
Only the voices from hell echo by her ear, clearer than ever.
Oni.
Vicious Oni.
Go to hell.
This place.
I picked this empty lot by the riverbank rather than those ruins along the way, so you won't be distracted by the memories. No need to thank me.
Good timing, too. The fireworks show isn't over yet.
What? Getting cold feet now?
No.
I just wanted to clear up everything before we started fighting.
What else isn't clear?
Only one thing.
Where did your hatred come from?
I ran into Tetsuya at the TV station and heard from him what happened when Uncle Tessai died. You didn't have to be so cruel.
I don't know how he treated you all these years, but he must not have seen you as an adversary if you got as far as to turn him into a figurehead.
Beside, I didn't think I did you any wrong. When I came back to Mitsukue, all I wanted was to make sure that my sacrifice wasn't meaningless—
Sacrifice?
That's why you make me sick, you and Tessai.
You're a fool, and he's an even bigger fool. Even if you really did accidentally kill the candidate, did you think he wouldn't know to frame me for it, since I was there too?
He favored you, so I kept waiting for the day when he would make me 'take responsibility' for the murder.
It would have been the best result. He would protect his favorite, while eliminating a subordinate who had been going over his head.
As for that little thing about killing one's brothers... what does it matter? Who would be foolish enough to speak up, with Hannya in your hand and his support behind you?
But he chose to cut ties with his favorite, choosing honor over family... how sickening.
Did he really not know that the oyabun of the Kinsekikai was the furthest thing from 'honor', or did he just pretend not to know?
Honor is a personal thing. When a group of people start acting in the name of honor, they'll either fall apart, or they're just dregs of society fooling themselves.
Tessai always liked to boast, talking about how yakuza were pathetic outcasts that could only live under the protection of the syndicate.
Retired veterans, hoodlums, gamblers, peddlers, orphans... Tessai united them under the banner of Kinsekikai, hoping they would love each other like family...

Forget everything before that night for a moment and let's just talk about what happened after the diet candidate's death. Shimasu tried to blackmail me, Gouda tried to undermine me, and Sanoguchi tried to exclude me as he once excluded you...
Family? Nothing more than old Tessai's pipe dream.
Besides, is it anything more than just an excuse for one's failure to fit into society? What do you say, Officer Hoshiguma? If what he said was true, then what are you, and what am I?
If this is your idea of fitting into society, I'm glad I was the one who took responsibility.
Finally, you're making sense, Officer Hoshiguma. You have no idea how lucky you are.
You took responsibility and left. I chose silence and stayed, cleaning up the mess that you and Tessai left behind.
Are the public up in arms about the yakuza killing a Diet candidate? Then spend money, build industries and tread on your own dignity to butter up real Diet members.
Getting hit by TV reports? Build your own media through crystalline industry, and change the image of the yakuza.
People starting to have designs on your burgeoning industry? Set your pride aside, and cling to the people who have the most use of you.
Run into someone that you can't soothe over? Then there's no choice. Eliminate them.
And you're complaining about it? Didn't you choose it yourself?
Yes, I chose it. I let my own weakness shut my own mouth that night!
You chose the twisted path of shame and guilty conscience—
Guilty conscience?!
Don't make me laugh, Officer Hoshiguma!
The path I chose showed just how incompetent Tessai was, how hypocritical his honor was, and how close his Kinsekikai was to destruction!
He was an incompetent, blind shogi player who began to abandon piece after piece for his precious Kinsekikai as changing times threatened to checkmate it.
Your incident was just the beginning. The concept of 'Diet' had just emerged, and he had already abandoned you.

When the relationship between North and South was at its most delicate, he was foolish enough to buy weapons from Northern Court factions, just to destroy a gang that was acting out of line in his eyes, one that had better weapons than the Kinsekikai.
He didn't even have that much of a grudge against them, other than the fact that they had once harbored the 'Wandering Vicious Oni' who killed his family.
Ostensibly, he was taking revenge for his wife and son. And look what came of it! He abandoned Tetsuya's mother and turned Tetsuya against him.
As for me? How many times would he have abandoned me, but for the fact that this lieutenant of his never believed in this 'honor' of his for even a moment?
I was proven right, time and again. He's no more than a Pawn who thinks himself a King, charging blindly into the fray...
Did he think promotion awaited? No, everyone else, friend or foe, was walking in the opposite direction. There is nothing but a dead end awaiting him at the end of the road.
To hold back the tide for that fool, clean up his mess, all the while getting walked all over by him?
I would rather you had killed me in your madness that night!
Happy now? Is that enough reason for you to bring your sword or shield to bear?
I only have one question. Are you putting on an act?
...An act?
I agree that he could have reinforced his position by putting the blame on you.
I can understand why you say that the oyabun of the Kinsekikai is the furthest from honor.
I believe you when you say you stemmed the tide of Kinsekikai's destruction, and that you are angry at Uncle Tessai for being in the way.
Then what do you want to say?
I just don't know what to think anymore.
It was you who brought me back to Kajimachi. It was you who tried to make me lose my reason and eliminate me once and for all.
It was you who earned Tetsuya's fervent loyalty. It was you who made him witness his own father's death at your hands.
It was you who cut off your own finger in front of Uncle Tessai. It was you who cursed his incompetence, even unto his death.
Your analysis was very clear, very rational. I fully understood what you were saying, and how you got to this point.
But the clearer and more rational it is, the less I could believe it...
